 If you’re looking to join a growth-driven environment that pairs innovation with profitability and reliability, welcome to Nordiska. We look forward to receiving your application.
   

  

 We review and interview continuously, with background checks as part of the final stage of our process. For questions about the role and or more information, you are warmly welcome to contact Ida Garamvölgyi at ida.garamvolgyi@nordiska.com or +46704-385 325.

title: Senior Frontend Developer
description:
We’re looking for a Senior Frontend Engineer for our client in Stockholm!
 



  





  





**The role** 




 You'll be working with an exciting deep-tech start-up building a cutting-edge collaboration platform for the manufacturing industry, working at the frontier of engineering, cloud-native platforms, and intelligent design automation.
 



  





 The work consists of high-performance, real-time product interfaces used by expert users to explore complex designs, collaborate live, and make critical decisions. This is a high-agency role with real architectural ownership and direct impact on how the product evolves.
 



  





**What you’ll be doing** 



* Building core product interfaces using React, Next.js, and TypeScript
* Designing and maintaining reusable, accessible UI systems
* Implementing real-time collaboration features over WebSockets
* Working closely with product and design to ship intuitive, polished experiences
* Optimising rendering performance, asset loading, and perceived latency in complex UIs



  





**Requirements** 



* 5+ years of experience building modern frontend applications in React.
* Deep understanding of component architecture, performance, and accessibility
* Product mindset: you care about how things are used, not just how they’re built
* Proven experience working in a fast-paced, high-ownership, environment.
* Fluent English Speaker



  





**Bonus** 
 - Any experience with WebGL, WebGPU, or 3D-adjacent frontend work
 



  





**What's on offer** 



* Above market base salary
* ITP Pension model
* 30 days vacation
* Stock Option package

title: Senior .NET Developer
description:
We now have a new requirement and are looking for a consultant colleague with the qualifications listed below. The position is part of our consulting business, which means you will be employed by us and work either with clients or on internal projects and assignments.
   

  

*As part of your application, you may be presented to clients/partners as part of the recruitment process. If selected for the assignment, you will be employed by us and work either with clients or on internal projects and assignments as part of our consulting business.*
**Your Profile (Must-have Requirements)**
* You have 10+ years of experience working with modern backend development practices, with focus on .NET Frameworks and C#.
* You have good experience in cloud environments
* You have good experience working with SQL databases
* You are familiar with working with microservices & event driven architecture
* You have an understanding and interest in building and maintaining microservices running in Kubernetes
* You have experience with build tools and services such as AzureDevops


**Application deadline:** 
 05-05-2026
   

  

**About Us**
 We currently employ experienced consultants in areas such as System Development, AI, Machine Learning, Testing, Cloud, Infra DevOps, and IT Project Management.
   

  

 Working at Deploja is something entirely different from working at an ordinary consulting company—and we are really proud of that. So, what makes us unique?
   

  

 We simply offer more than just a high salary and greater freedom; that’s just the beginning. With us, you’ll find the work environment and opportunities you deserve. Welcome to Deploja, where the grass is indeed greener!
   

  

**Vi erbjuder dig följande, Valet är ditt!**
 🚀 Exciting assignments with our many clients. We focus on long-term projects, which gives you the opportunity to create value for the client while also developing yourself.
   

  

 🌟 A secure monthly salary – together we’ll agree on a base salary depending on your assignments.
   

  

 🚗 Company car – we encourage you to choose an electric car for a more sustainable future.
   

  

 💼 Occupational pension and great opportunities for salary conversion.
   

  

 🌴 Vacation – Would you like more than 30 days of vacation, perhaps 60 days?
   

  

 🏥 Private health insurance, accident insurance, and life insurance for increased security.
   

  

 📚 Skills development – you set your own budget and choose the training you wish to attend.
   

  

 🕑 Quality of life – Are you struggling to balance everyday life and wish to work a little less while still earning as much, if not more?
   

  

*We handle applications continuously, and due to the high workload, it's especially important to include a short motivation for the position, explaining how you meet the requirements.*

title: Developer
description:
Scania is undergoing a transformation from being a leading supplier of trucks, buses, and engines to providing complete and sustainable transport solutions. Together with TRATON and our sister brands , we are working to shape the future of mobility with innovative and environmentally conscious solutions. Our values – customer first, respect, team spirit, responsibility, and elimination of waste – are at the heart of everything we do. Together, we are at the forefront of creating a sustainable future.
   

  

**Role Summary**
 As a developer within the Diagnostic Platform, you will be part of cross-functional teams working to design and develop the next generation of tools for distributing software to vehicles. You will be working closely with other teams located in Sweden, Germany and the US.
   

  

 The role places great emphasis on your communication skills and your ability to independently take responsibility and identify needs.
   

  

**Job Responsibilities**
* Work and in agile teams and contribute to both the team and your own development.
* Collaborate within a cross-functional, agile product team.
* Design, develop, test, and maintain services using C#/C+.
* Build and manage AWS infrastructure.
* Improve monitoring, logging, and observability to ensure system reliability and performance.
* Participate in architectural discussions and technical planning.


**Who You Are**
* University degree or equivalent experience.
* 5+ years of software development experience.
* Independently working with a high sense of ownership of the product.
* Experienced in dotnet .NET or equivalent.
* Experience working with AWS.
* Strong communication skills and a collaborative attitude.


**Bonus:**
* Experience in Vehicle Diagnostics.
* Experience with setting up monitoring/observability tooling (e.g., OpenTelemetry, Grafana, Prometheus).


**This Is Us**
 The Diagnostic Platform is responsible for creating and supporting services for distributing vehicle software. our customers are both production and aftermarket.
   

  

 We believe in teamwork, knowledge sharing, and collaboration across borders and functions. With curiosity and a continuous improvement mindset, we work together to develop solutions that make a real difference for our customers and for our future.
   

  

**Scania Offers**
 We offer a dynamic, flexible workplace with hybrid work options, including Scania Sergel and Midway hubs. With a structured development plan and courses, Scania supports your career growth both locally and internationally.
   

  

 Benefits include training at our health center Gröndal or wellness allowance, result bonus, flexible hours, and company car leasing. Scania also hosts events for employees and their families, and Stockholm residents enjoy direct access to Södertälje via Scania Job express buses.
   

  

**Application**
 Your application should include a CV and any relevant certificates. We welcome all applicants and strive for diversity in our recruitment processes.
   

  

 Apply as soon as possible, no later than 2026-05-18. Screening will take place on an ongoing basis during the application period. Logical and personality tests may be used as part of the selection process, and a background check may be required for this role.
   

  

 If you have questions or would like more information, please contact:
   

  

 Kenneth Gylling, Group Manager, kenneth.gylling@scania.com
   

  

 We look forward to your application!

title: System Developer
description:
Acne Studios is a progressive luxury house and through collaboration and curiosity, we set high aspirations and strive for excellence. We value everyone’s contribution and embrace feedback to develop ourselves and others. We aim to minimize our environmental impact across all our operations and to address societal challenges in the way we conduct our business.
   

  

 As a System Developer in our Stockholm headquarters, you’ll join a skilled development team working at the intersection of fashion, retail, and technology. This role is ideal for someone with 1-2 years of experience in Dynamics 365 F&O and Azure integrations who is ready to take the next step and deepen their expertise in a dynamic, global environment.
   

  

 You’ll collaborate closely with senior developers, testers, IT Business Partners, and teams across the company. Together, you’ll design, build, and maintain solutions that support our global operations - from purchasing and logistics to retail and finance. Our tech stack includes Dynamics 365 F&O, Azure integration services, and Azure DevOps.
   

  

 CULTURE OF COLLABORATION
   

  

 We firmly believe that when you feel supported, included, and trusted, it enables you to enjoy your work and thrive in it. You will be a part of a team of colleagues with diverse roles and backgrounds, each with their unique skills and experiences.
   

  

 WHAT YOU WILL DO
   

  

 As System Developer your main contribution will be to build and maintain solutions that support our global operations. You will report to the Head of System Development & Business Relations. You will play a vital role in the development team where you will:
   

  

* Develop, test, deploy, and maintain software solutions and integrations that align with our architectural and coding standards
* Grow your craft with coaching from experienced senior developers
* Contribute to solution design and take an active role in technical discussions
* Write clean, structured, and well‑documented code
* Participate in code reviews and offer constructive, thoughtful feedback
* Troubleshoot and resolve defects, performance challenges, and technical debt
* Partner with IT Business Partners to assess needs and estimate development efforts
* Provide third‑line support and help diagnose production issues
* Collaborate closely with QA to refine testing practices and shape effective test strategies
* Highlight risks, gaps, and potential business impact throughout development
* Continuously seek ways to improve our tools, processes, and systems
* Share knowledge generously and contribute to a collaborative, learning‑focused culture


 WHO YOU ARE
   

  

 You have high aspirations and focus on details that create substantial impact. You enjoy working as a team and you embrace feedback to develop yourself and others. You are curious and thrive in a dynamic environment. Cooperation, respect, and clear communication are important, along with your ambition to learn and grow. If these qualities speak to you, we would love to have you on our team.
   

  

 Specific personal qualities for this role:
   

  

* You enjoy solving problems and understanding how systems support business processes
* You’re driven by improving how things work and creating clarity in complexity
* You feel comfortable asking questions, sharing ideas, and collaborating closely with others
* You’re eager to grow your skills - and you value a workplace where clarity, simplicity, and quality truly matter


 Specific experience and knowledge for this role:
   

  

* 1-2 years of hands‑on experience with Dynamics 365 F&O development (X++)
* Experience with Azure integrations is a strong plus
* A solid understanding of software development principles and coding standards
* Experience working in - or supporting - processes within retail is an advantage
* A structured, solution‑oriented approach to work
* Curiosity and an interest in AI, automation, and innovation
* A collaborative mindset and a genuine interest in learning and contributing
* Fluency in English


 What matters most to us is not whether you master all the listed knowledge and skills. We are dedicated to recognising the potential in each individual. We embrace diversity of people as well as ideas and viewpoints and encourage everyone's individual expression of their own identity.

title: Senior Backend Developer
description:
In this role you will work on various development projects focused on delivering new and updated features for players, Paradox game studios, and internal tools. You will be part of a team tasked with designing, building, and shaping the effort in realizing features which enrich our Paradox ecosystem. This also means that you will be working closely with all of the Paradox game development studios, as well as the publishing organization, in order to maximize the consumption of our services to guarantee we continue delivering Paradox signature experiences.
   

  

**Goals**
* Help the Product Owner, by providing technical expertise, in establishing, defining and ongoing refinement of the Product Roadmap.
* Collaborating with Technical Leader in the successful delivery of the committed tasks
* Working with Team members, contribute to the satisfactory resolution of all Stories in the Product Backlog.
* Working with the Management, ensure your expectations align with Paradoxes expectations and values.


**Relationships**
* The Developer will report to his/her Manager
* The Developer helps the Product Management understand the technical requirements for completing tasks
* Consultation and collaboration in achieving the committed goals of the Team
* Synchronize daily with the Technical Lead to report on the progress of committed tasks


**Responsibilities**
* Responsibilities include but are not limited to:
* Generalized JavaScript development in frontend and backend.
* Build and maintain JavaScript services, scripts, and cloud functions
* Build and maintain internal JavaScript libraries and utilities.
* Maintain current and develop new products and/or services applicable to the Employees Team
* Provide internal support for the teams services


**Qualifications**
* Experience with NodeJS.
* Experience with modern JavaScript (ES2018+).
* Experience with databases (SQL, non-relational dbs like DynamoDB).
* Experience running services on cloud providers like AWS or GCP
* Experience with containers or containerised setups
* General understanding of best practices for data caching and writing APIs.
* General understanding of how backend APIs work (CRUD, REST, etc.).
* General understanding of authentication standards (OIDC / OAuth)
* Test-oriented mindset with unit and/or integration tests written using standard libraries
* An understanding of and practical experience with Agile methodologies.
* Excellent English communication skills
* Service-minded
* Supplemental qualifications
* Experience in C++, C#, Typescript, React, Go and/or Python
* Experience with infrastructure as code (Terraform)
* Experience with distributed systems non-monolithic services


**Scope:** 
 Full-time and permanent, with a 6 months probation period
   

  

**Reports to:** 
 Online Service Manager, Central Tech & Services
   

  

**Location:** 
 Onsite, Stockholm, Sweden

title: Dot net developer lead
description:
About the job
 
  





**Company Description** 




 Wiselink Global Services Pvt Limited is a technology-driven organization specializing in business solutions, consulting, and outsourcing services. The company is committed to delivering high-quality, timely, and innovative solutions to organizations across the globe. Focusing on efficiency and excellence, Wiselink Global ensures that its services meet the needs of its diverse client base. Their mission centers around empowering businesses with cutting-edge technology and customer-centric solutions.
 



  





**Role Description** 




 We are seeking a skilled Dotnet Developer for a full-time, on-site role based in Madrid. The Dotnet Developer will be responsible for designing, developing, and implementing software solutions using .NET technologies. Day-to-day responsibilities include writing, testing, and maintaining code in line with business requirements, collaborating with development teams, and ensuring high-quality deliverables. The ideal candidate will also be tasked with troubleshooting and optimizing applications to enhance performance and functionality.
 



  





**Qualifications** 



* .
 **Net Framework (C#, ASP.NET), L2/L3 support ,ITIL processes and ITSM tools (ServiceNow or equivalent), Handling 24x7 operations, critical incidents, Monitoring & observability tools (APM, logs, alerts)**

title: Software Engineer
description:
**Software Developer – Trading Systems**
 As a Software Developer, you'll play a critical role in building and maintaining one of Nasdaq’s core systems powering trading at the heart of Nasdaq Stockholm.
   

  

 You'll thrive in this position if you're detail-oriented, collaborative, and passionate about working on complex systems where precision and reliability are paramount. This is a long-term opportunity to grow deep expertise in financial technology alongside a stable, experienced team.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Participate in all phases of the lifecycle of software application development with an emphasis on architecture, design, quality, code reviews and implementation
* Develop and maintain backend systems that process real-time equity trades with ultra-low latency requirements.
* Write clean, well-tested code and conduct thorough code reviews to ensure system reliability and quality.
* Collaborate with a small, focused team of developers to deliver enhancements to critical trading infrastructure.
* Troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues using data analysis, scripting, and network diagnostics.
* Contribute to system architecture decisions with a focus on scalability, maintainability, and performance.


**Required Qualifications**
* Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
* 2–3+ years of experience in software development, ideally in backend or systems programming.
* Proficiency in Java and object-oriented programming principles and design patterns.
* Solid understanding of Linux/Unix environments
* Excellent attention to detail and a structured approach to problem-solving in high-stakes environments.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Understanding of network protocols and ability to analyze network traffic (e.g., tcpdumps).
* Background in financial services, capital markets, or fintech.


 This position will be located in Stockholm and offers the opportunity for a hybrid work environment at least 3 days a week in-office, subject to change, providing flexibility and accessibility for qualified candidates.
   

  

**Come as You Are**
 Nasdaq is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds and identities. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace where diverse perspectives, experiences, and identities are valued and celebrated. We ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided with reasonable accommodation throughout the hiring process. To request accommodation, contact us directly.
   

  

**Benefits & Rewards**
 We offer a competitive, well-rounded rewards package that supports you and your family - inside and outside work.
   

  

 Actual pay depends on your skills, experience, education, and location. In addition to base pay, we offer short-term incentives (bonus or commission) and long-term incentives (equity), where applicable, as well as the following benefits:
   

  

**What We Offer**
* Competitive base salary
* Annual bonus
* Annual equity grant
* Employee Stock Purchase Plan offering discounted company shares
* Pension plan
* 32 paid vacation days
* 6 additional days off per year
* Work from (almost) anywhere – up to 20 days/year
* Paid time off to volunteer
* Health insurance
* Friskvårdsbidrag/Wellness Allowance
* 24/7 mental health support for you and your family
* Global mentoring program
* Unlimited access to e-learning platforms
* Hybrid work setup


 For more information, visit Nasdaq Benefits & Rewards Career Page.
   

  

**Come as You Are**
 Nasdaq is an equal opportunity employer. We positively encourage applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of age, color, disability, national origin, ancestry, race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, veteran status, genetic information, or any other status protected by applicable law.
   

  

 We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request an accommodation.

title: Senior Fullstack Engineer - Commerce Platform
description:
The Subscriptions Mission builds and evolves the experiences and systems that allow millions of listeners to discover, subscribe to, and enjoy Spotify Premium. Our teams focus on awareness, acquisition, activation, retention, and commerce — ensuring seamless experiences that help fans connect more deeply with the audio they love while enabling sustainable growth for Spotify.
   

  

 The Commerce Platform powers the purchasing and subscription systems that enable millions of users worldwide to access Spotify Premium. Within this area, the Payment SDK squad is responsible for the technology that handles initial payment transactions across our platform.
   

  

 As a Senior Fullstack Engineer, you’ll work on systems that process large volumes of transactions and support the global purchase experience. Your work will help evolve the platform that powers Spotify’s commerce ecosystem, ensuring our payment infrastructure remains scalable, reliable, and innovative as we grow.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Architect and implement scalable backend services and frontend components that power Spotify’s commerce platform.
* Design and develop critical capabilities in Java and Python services operating in a high-volume, low-latency environment.
* Build and maintain the pay-in functionality that enables payment processing across Spotify products.
* Run and analyze experiments to improve payment performance and user experience.
* Improve system reliability through monitoring, alerting, and operational best practices.
* Collaborate with engineers, product managers, and cross-functional teams to deliver impactful commerce features.
* Contribute to evolving Spotify’s payment infrastructure and SDK capabilities for developers across the company.


 Who You Are
   

  

* You are experienced with modern JavaScript development, testing, and debugging.
* You have experience building and scaling backend services using Java and/or Python.
* You have worked in cloud-native environments such as Google Cloud Platform or similar platforms.
* You have experience designing and maintaining libraries, frameworks, or developer tools used by other engineers.
* You have hands-on experience working with scalable databases such as PostgreSQL.
* You approach engineering problems with strong analytical thinking and practical solutions.
* You communicate clearly and collaborate well with cross-functional teams.
* Experience working in e-commerce, financial systems, or high-volume transaction environments is a plus.


 Where You'll Be
   

  

* This role is based in London or Stockholm.
* We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.


 Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
   

  

 At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.
   

  

 Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

title: AI Developer
description:
**Role description**

 LTM Limited, formerly known as LTIMindtree  — a Larsen & Toubro company — is an AI-centric global technology services company and the Business Creativity partner to the world’s largest and most disruptive enterprises. We bring human insights and intelligent systems together to help clients create greater value at the intersection of technology and domain expertise. Our capabilities span integrated operations, transformation, and business AI — enabling new ways of working, new productivity paradigms, and new roads to value. Together with over 87,000 employees across 40 countries and our global network of partners, LTM owns business outcomes for our clients, helping them not just outperform the market, but to Outcreate it. 
   

 http://ltm.com
 


  


 We are looking for an AI Developer in Sodertalje, Sweden with below expertise.
 


  


 Mode of Employment: Fixed Term Contract / B2B Contract
 


  


 Duration : 6-12 months
 


  


 Hybrid Model
 


  



**Job Description** 




  





  




 ·
 
         
 


 Be hands-on in Python or TypeScript and comfortable building production grade backend systems
 


  




 ·
 
         
 


 Have solid experience with Docker and cloud deployment on AWS ECS Fargate S3 RDS Cognito
 


  




 ·
 
         
 


 Be capable of managing infrastructures code Terraform and CICD pipelines GitLab
 


  




 ·
 
         
 


 Understand LLMs and agent-based systems including how AI agents interact with tools and data
 


  




 ·
 
         
 


 Have experience or strong exposure to RAG pipelines vector search and enterprise data querying SQL
 


  




 ·
 
         
 


 Be able to design and operate AI agents not just write prompts focusing on accuracy performance cost and reliability
 


  




 ·
 
         
 


 Work comfortably across the stack from cloud infra and data layers to agent logic and workflows
 


  


**Nice to have strengths include** 



  




 o
 
   
 


 Agent prompt engineering and governance focused AI development
 


  




 o
 
   
 


 Enterprise integrations ERP finance systems identity SSO
 


  




 o
 
   
 


 Analytics query federation or finance domain exposure
 


  




 o
 
   
 


 Awareness of AI governance compliance and observability

title: Engineering Manager
description:
**WHO WE ARE** 





 At Trustly, we're building a smarter, faster, and more secure financial future by revolutionizing the world of payments. As a global leader in Open Banking Payments, we are establishing Pay by Bank as the new standard at checkout, providing unparalleled freedom, speed, and ease to millions of consumers and merchants worldwide.
 




  






 Our Ambition: To build the world’s most disruptive payment network and redefine what the payment experience should feel like.
 




  






 Trustly is a global team of innovators, collaborators, and doers.  If you are driven by a strong sense of purpose and thrive in a dynamic, entrepreneurial, and high-growth environment, join us and be part of a team that’s transforming the way the world pays.
 




  





**About the Product & Tech Organisation** 



  


  
 


  



 Our Product & Technology organisation drives the development of Trustly’s products. The cross functional teams collectively work with innovative ideas, development of products and platform and set the vision and strategy for the problem spaces they own. The Product & Tech organisation consists of a bit more than 200 people spread across Stockholm, Lisbon, Örebro, Paris, London and Izmir. 
 



  


  
 


  



 All teams consist of a product manager, an engineering manager, a lead developer and engineers. Some teams also have designers and analytics competence in the team. They all work closely together to enable new products and features within existing products within Europe and to make sure the payment platform runs without interruptions and with high performance.
 



  


  
 


  



 We are now looking to strengthen our engineering capacity within Product & Technology with one more senior position. We operate in an agile culture combined with DevOps, allowing our teams to take on full responsibility from code to on-call.
 



  


  
 


  


**About the Role** 



  


  
 


  



 As an
 
**Engineering Manager** 

 at Trustly, you will play a crucial role in leading and supporting an agile team of talented engineers. Working closely with product managers, lead developers, and other stakeholders, you’ll ensure delivery of high-quality, scalable, and reliable systems while fostering a positive and empowering team culture. This is an excellent opportunity to bridge technical excellence and people management, delivering real impact in the payments industry.
 





  




**What you'll do** 
  
* **Team Leadership** 
 : Lead, coach, and support a team of engineers, enabling them to perform at their best while fostering a culture of collaboration, ownership, and continuous learning
  
* **Delivery Excellence** 
 : Drive successful delivery of the team’s commitments, ensuring alignment with domain objectives and product goals
  
* **Technical Collaboration** 
 : Work hands-on with lead developers and engineers to design, implement, and maintain scalable systems and solutions aligned with Trustly’s technical standards
  
* **Product Partnership** 
 : Work closely with the product manager to define and prioritise the team’s roadmap, balancing short-term goals with long-term vision
  
* **Operational Ownership** 
 : Ensure your team’s systems operate reliably in production, with high performance, availability, and maintainability
  
* **Talent Development** 
 : Recruit, onboard, and mentor engineers, creating an environment where they can grow and thrive
  
* **Process Improvement** 
 : Advocate for and implement best practices in agile methodologies, DevOps, and engineering processes to continuously improve team effectiveness
  

  



  




**Who you are** 
  
* Proven experience managing engineering teams in agile environments, with a strong focus on team empowerment and delivery
  
* Hands-on engineering background with expertise in Java and experience in building scalable, robust systems
  
* Familiarity with modern tech stacks including Kubernetes, Docker, Kafka, and Spring Boot
  
* Experience working with cloud solutions (preferably AWS), monitoring, and analytics
  
* Knowledge of service-based architecture and microservices
  
* Strong ability to balance technical decisions with business needs, ensuring delivery without sacrificing scalability, performance, or reliability
  
* Committed to fostering a culture of collaboration, innovation, and excellence
  
* Passion for DevOps practices and ownership, with a track record of driving improvements in automation and monitoring
  
* Exceptional communication, problem-solving, and leadership skills
  
* Fluent in English
  

  



  




**At Trustly, you’ll have the opportunity to:** 
  
* Shape the future of payments as part of a global leader in the industry
  
* Work with cutting-edge technology and highly skilled colleagues in an agile and collaborative environment
  
* Take on a role with real impact, where your contributions directly drive the success of your team and Trustly’s mission
  
* Enjoy a supportive and dynamic culture that encourages growth, innovation, and ownership
  

  






  




  


**Our Fantastic Benefits**

 (varies by location)
 




 🌴
 **20 to 30 days of holiday** 
 to support a healthy work-life balance
 



 🥳
 **Monthly team outing allowance** 
 to enjoy social events with your colleagues
 



 👶
 **Parental leave top-up** 
 additional support for new parents
 



 🥐
 **Daily breakfast and on-site perks** 
 to make your workday smoother
 



 💚
 **Well being support** 
 our health allowance covers gym memberships, massages, and much more to help you feel your best
 



**PLUS** 
 additional benefits designed to enhance your work-life experience!
 



  





**Shape Your Role and Make an Impact**




 Join a dynamic environment where you can take ownership, drive change, and continuously embrace new challenges. We’re looking for dedicated and highly motivated individuals who thrive in a fast-paced environment and enjoy collaborating across different areas of the organisation.
 



 If your skills and experience align with this role, we’d love to hear from you!
 **Apply now and submit your CV in English.** 



  


 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Digital Developer Site Respiratory - Sweden Operations
description:
*We have an exciting opportunity to welcome our next
 **Digital Developer** 
 to the Technology organisation within Site Respiratory!*
 Welcome to Sweden Operations, here the pulse is high and the possibility to develop is high. We work sustainably for each other and the planet in a high-tech and positive environment. Coaching leadership and curiosity, drive us towards finding new ways to manufacture medicine that change lives.
   

  

 Within Site Respiratory, pharmaceutical products for close to 20 million patients are filled and packed in over 100 different markets. We have a very exciting future and we will, among other things, launch several new products, invest in new production equipment and at the same time improve our business to world class. Here, about 470 employees work daily to manufacture mainly inhalation products for patients worldwide.
   

  

**Your role**
 As a Digital Developer, you are responsible to execute the digital strategy, lead the cultural journey and build digital competence and agile working methods to accelerate the business forward.
   

  

**Main Duties And Responsibilities**
* Identify opportunities for innovation, improve working methods and processes to simplify and optimize our business
* Interact with various stakeholders and forums within SweOps as well as global colleagues and functions to further develop and execute the implementation of various initiatives and projects
* Increase knowledge and competence in areas that support site Respiratory's priorities and digital strategy
* Business intelligence with AI for optimization and decision making
* The transformation is conducted in cross-functional collaboration with business developers, digital networks and smart factory teams among others


**Essentials For The Role**
* Relevant academic education (e.g. computer science/IT, engineering/automation)
* Experience in agile and/or design thinking methods
* A great interest and commitment to people, digitalization, AI and technology as well as to challenge existing working methods and processes.
* Experience of facilitating, leading meetings and workshops
* Good communication skills in speech and writing (Swedish and English)


*This role could be either an GCL: E or a GCL: D, depending on your width of experience.*
**Desirable for the role**
* Experience from digital transformations and change management
* Competence in project management
* Knowledge in Lean, production systems and systematic improvement work


**Success factors**
 In order for you to thrive and succeed in this role, we also believe that you need to be self-motivated, curious, brave, innovative and responsive.
   

  

**Why AstraZeneca**
 When we at AstraZeneca see the opportunity to change, we seize the opportunity – because every opportunity, no matter how small, can be the start of something big. Delivering medicine that changes people's lives is about having an entrepreneurial spirit – finding the moments and understanding their potential.
   

  

 Therefore, we are looking for you who thrive in a dynamic and changing environment. Because through your work, you and your team will be able to make a difference to patients' lives every day as you ensure that the production of vital medicines can continue.
   

  

**Are you ready to make a difference, great then send your application – and we will do it together.**
 Please submit your application no later than
 **8th of May** 
 . Interviews may be held on an ongoing basis during the advertising period.
   

  

**Date Posted**
 28-apr.-2026
   

  

**Closing Date**
 08-maj-2026
   

  

 Our mission is to build an inclusive and equitable environment. We want people to feel they belong at AstraZeneca and Alexion, starting with our recruitment process. We welcome and consider applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of characteristics. We offer reasonable adjustments/accommodations to help all candidates to perform at their best. If you have a need for any adjustments/accommodations, please complete the section in the application form.

title: Senior AI Developer – LLM Fine-Tuning & Generative AI
description:
Vi söker nu en Senior AI Developer för ett spännande uppdrag inom avancerad generativ AI, där fokus ligger på träning, anpassning och finjustering av stora språkmodeller (LLMs) samt utveckling av robusta AI-lösningar för affärskritiska processer.
   

  

 I denna roll kommer du att arbeta nära verksamheten för att utveckla skräddarsydda språkmodeller där generella modeller inte är tillräckliga. Uppdraget innefattar fine-tuning av open-source LLM:er, optimering för edge-miljöer med höga säkerhetskrav samt utveckling av pipelines för effektiv deployment och inferens.
   

  

 Du blir en viktig del i ett tekniskt avancerat team där tidigare erfarenheter av reinforcement learning, modelloptimering och generativa AI-flöden tas vidare till nästa nivå.
   

  

**Exempel på arbetsuppgifter**
* Utföra supervised fine-tuning av LLM:er mot specifika domän- och uppgiftsdata
* Optimera modeller för edge-miljöer med hjälp av CUDA, Docker samt kvantiserings- och pruning-tekniker
* Utvärdera modellprestanda och jämföra mot basmodeller genom benchmarks och interna testmiljöer
* Bygga robusta pipelines för deployment i resursbegränsade miljöer
* Samarbeta med domänexperter för datainsamling, träningsdata och KPI-definition
* Dokumentera metodik, kod och resultat för långsiktig återanvändning och vidareutveckling


**Vi söker dig som har**
* Senior erfarenhet inom AI-utveckling med fokus på generativ AI och LLMs
* Djup kunskap inom fine-tuning, träning och optimering av språkmodeller
* Erfarenhet av open-source LLM:er och moderna GenAI-ramverk
* Stark kompetens inom Python och ML/AI-ekosystemet
* Erfarenhet av CUDA, Docker och deployment i edge-miljöer
* Förståelse för reinforcement learning, modellutvärdering och AI-pipelines
* Förmåga att arbeta självständigt och samtidigt samarbeta nära både teknik- och verksamhetsteam


 Det här är en möjlighet för dig som vill arbeta med AI på riktigt — där tekniken används för att lösa komplexa problem med höga krav på prestanda, säkerhet och kvalitet.
   

  

****Placering****
 Placering hos vår kund i Stockholm med möjlighet till hybrid remote. Vi ser att du är bosatt någonstans kring Stcokholm, Uppsala eller straxt norr därtill.

title: Summer
description:
Req ID:518659
   

  

 At Alstom, we understand transport networks and what moves people. From high-speed trains, metros, monorails, and trams, to turnkey systems, services, infrastructure, signalling and digital mobility, we offer our diverse customers the broadest portfolio in the industry. Every day, more than 80 000 colleagues lead the way to greener and smarter mobility worldwide, connecting cities as we reduce carbon and replace cars.
   

  

**Are you interested in getting an insight into what it’s like to work as a software developer on life‑saving, safety‑critical products in the railway industry?**
 The task for this temporary assignment is to recreate existing UML diagrams in the modeling tool Visual Paradigm. The original diagrams are available as image files and need to be accurately redrawn as editable UML models in Visual Paradigm.
   

  

 The diagrams created should closely match the originals. You’ll work with support and guidance from experienced colleagues whenever needed.
   

  

 The assignment is expected to lastup to five weeks.
   

  

**Required qualifications:**
* You are careful and pay attention to details.
* Basic knowledge of UML diagrams.


**Meriting qualifications:**
* Previous experience using Visual Paradigm or similar UML modeling tools.


 You will be working from our new modern office in Marievik in Liljeholmen, with the possibility to work from home a few days per week.
   

  

 You don’t need to be a train enthusiast to thrive with us. We guarantee that when you step onto one of our trains with your friends or family, you’ll be proud. If you’re up for the challenge, we’d love to hear from you!
   

  

**Important to note**
 As a global business, we’re an equal-opportunity employer that celebrates diversity across the 63 countries we operate in. We’re committed to creating an inclusive workplace for everyone.

title: Product Developer
description:
**Produktutvecklare till Primus-Silva i Stockholm** 




*En roll för dig som brinner för fantastiska produkter och friluftsliv!* 




  





*Vi söker en passionerad och driven Produktutvecklare som vill bli en del av vårt team och arbeta med konsumentprodukter för outdoor-segmentet. Som Produktutvecklare med ett starkt intresse för friluftsliv kommer du att spela en nyckelroll i att utveckla innovativa produkter av högsta kvalitet som gör det möjligt för människor att utforska världen!* 




***Placering:***
*PRIMUS-SILVA AB, huvudkontor i Bromma, Stockholm* 




  





**Din roll!** 




 I denna roll blir du en del av PRIMUS produktutvecklingsteam, som består av ingenjörer, produktutvecklare, förpackningsdesigners och industridesigners. Du rapporterar till PRIMUS R&D Manager. Ditt huvudsakliga fokus är att vidareutveckla befintliga produkter samt utveckla nya som stärker vårt erbjudande.
 



 Våra utvecklingsprocesser sträcker sig över cirka två år, där du antingen deltar i eller driver projekt från idé till färdig produkt.
 



 Vi befinner oss i en spännande fas där vi fortsätter att investera i ny produktutveckling och utmanar oss själva att tänka nytt, förbättras kontinuerligt och ligga i framkant inom vår kategori. I denna roll får du en viktig del i att driva den utvecklingen framåt.
 



 Du tar ägarskap för projekt och säkerställer att de följer uppsatta processer, tidsplaner och kvalitetskrav, samtidigt som du koordinerar tvärfunktionella team och löser tekniska utmaningar längs vägen.
 



  





**Dina ansvarsområden:** 



* **Projektledning & samarbete:** 
 Agera huvudkontakt mellan PRIMUS och leverantörer i dina projekt. Ansvara för hela projektets livscykel – från koncept till produktion – och säkerställa att arbetet följer PRIMUS utvecklingsprocess, tidsplan och kvalitetskrav. Koordinera arbetet mellan Product Managers, designers, ingenjörer och leverantörer, där du fungerar som navet i kommunikationen och driver projekten framåt. Vid behov eskalerar du kritiska frågor för att säkerställa leverans.
* **Utveckling & tekniskt bidrag:** 
 Bidra aktivt i utvecklingsarbetet med innovativa tekniska lösningar. Översätta marknads- och produktkrav till tydliga tekniska specifikationer och prestandakrav. Ta fram proof-of-principle-prototyper för att testa och utveckla koncept samt arbeta praktiskt med test och verifiering för att säkerställa produktens funktion och kvalitet.
* **Leverantörssamarbete:** 
 Bygga starka relationer med leverantörer, främst i Asien, och driva samarbetet för att säkerställa producerabarhet och hög produktkvalitet. Resor är en naturlig del av rollen (cirka 40 övernattningar per år).
* **Dokumentation & struktur:** 
 Ansvara för och vidareutveckla produktdokumentation, inklusive Product Definition Documents (PDD). Säkerställa att tekniska beslut, förändringar och utvecklingssteg dokumenteras tydligt. Följa upp och rapportera projektstatus, risker och nyckeltal samt säkerställa att produktdata är korrekt och uppdaterad i PIM-systemet.



  





**Vem vi söker!** 



* Minst kandidatexamen som ingenjör eller motsvarande område
* Erfarenhet från en liknande roll, gärna med en portfölj av konsumentprodukter i material som plast, metall, trä eller annan hårdvara
* Praktisk erfarenhet av outsourcad produktutveckling, gärna mot Asien, samt vana att arbeta nära externa partners



  





 Din personlighet är dock det viktigaste. Vår kultur är stark och vi söker dig som passar in hos oss.
 



 Du gillar att testa nytt och trivs med utmaningar. Du är strukturerad, noggrann och tar stort ansvar för ditt arbete. Samtidigt är du en lagspelare som är lyhörd för andras idéer och förstår att de bästa produkterna skapas tillsammans.
 



 PRIMUS-SILVA är ett bolag!
 



  





 Sedan maj 2023 ägs PRIMUS-SILVA av Verdane. SILVA och PRIMUS har båda en stark svensk historia och en lång tradition av innovation.
 



 SILVA uppfann den vätskefyllda kompassen 1933, vilket satte standarden för navigation globalt. Cirka 40 år tidigare uppfann PRIMUS grundare världens första sotfria fotogenkök.
 



 Idag är vi ett bolag med gemensam organisation och funktioner, samtidigt som vi behåller två starka och tydliga varumärken.
 




 Är du inte övertygad ännu besök vår hemsida här
 




  





**Sök rollen redan idag!** 




 Du är varmt välkommen att ladda upp ditt CV, alternativt med din LinkedIn URL. Vi tar inte emot ansökningar via mail. Urval kan ske löpande och tjänsten kan tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdag 10/5 2026, så vänta inte med din ansökan!
 



 För att garantera en rättvis och icke-diskriminerande rekryteringsprocess tar vi inte emot personliga brev. Istället får utvalda relevanta kandidater svara skriftligt på samma frågor för att säkerställa lika möjligheter.
 



 I denna rekrytering samarbetar Primus-Silva med ROI Rekrytering. Vid frågor kontakta rekryteringskonsult Lina Friberg på
 
 lina.friberg@roirekrytering.se
 
 .

title: SOFTWARE ENGINEER INOM FULLSTACK TILL AXFOOD IT
description:
**Är du i början av din karriär, vill jobba med utveckling och har ett särskilt intresse för data? Nu finns chansen att bli en del av Axfood Tech Development Program med start i augusti 2026 – ett ettårigt talangprogram riktat mot nyutexaminerade techtalanger. Genom att börja din karriär med ett talangprogram får du den ultimata övergången mellan studier och arbetsliv.**
**Att jobba med tech på Axfood**
 Axfoods syfte är att skapa bättre livskvalitet till alla och har visionen att vara ledande inom prisvärd, bra och hållbar mat. Som en av dagligvarubranschens största aktörer gör Axfoods olika bolag stor skillnad i miljontals människors liv – varje dag. Var med på en resa i en spännande bransch där nytänkande, engagemang är viktiga ingredienser.
   

  

 Dagligvarubranschen befinner sig i ett skifte med digitalisering och automation högt på agendan. Genom att arbeta både snabbare och mer strategiskt med data kan Axfood möta en ökad konkurrens och nya krav från kunder.
   

  

 Du blir en del av Axfood IT som har en av matbranschens största IT-avdelningar med drygt 300 medarbetare. Här får man vara med i arbetet som ökar effektiviteten i organisationen genom ett mer datadrivet arbetssätt och fortsätter utveckla framtidens logistiklösningar. Axfood IT ligger i framkant vad gäller digital utveckling och ny teknik inom områden som det digitala kundmötet, e-handel och arbetssätt.
   

  

**Rollen som Software Engineer**
 Som utvecklare ingår du i ett av teamen inom Business Analytics IT. Avdelningen består av cirka 22 medarbetare som säkerställer en stabil, säker och skalbar teknisk förmåga för analys. Tillsammans bidrar ni till att skapa en tillförlitlig och kvalitetssäkrad datagrund som möjliggör insikter, smarta beslut och affärsnytta för hela organisationen.
   

  

 Teamen hanterar en variation av uppgifter som kommer från projekt, vidareutveckling, incidenter och förvaltning. Arbetet bedrivs i agila sprintar om fyra veckor där uppgifter planeras och fördelas i början av varje sprint.
   

  

 Du kommer bland annat att:
   

  

* Utveckla och förvalta data pipelines
* Arbeta med datamodellering
* Bidra till front-end utveckling kopplat till analys och rapportering
* Hantera incidenter som en del av förvaltningsansvaret
* Bidra till förbättringar och effektivisering av arbetssätt


**Talangprogram – bryggan mellan studier och karriär**
 Nexer Tech Talent är specialister på att ge unga techtalanger den ultimata starten på arbetslivet. Vi skräddarsyr talangprogram som ger en sömlös övergång mellan studier och karriär där arbete kombineras med utbildning, handledning och sociala aktiviteter.
   

  

 Som deltagare i ett talangprogram kommer du att vara anställd hos Nexer Tech Talent men ha en roll som Applikationsutvecklare på Axfood i Stockholm. Du blir en del av en grupp talanger som alla deltar i ett skräddarsytt utbildningsprogram där ni månadsvis får möjlighet att utveckla och fördjupa era tekniska kunskaper i kombination med utbildning inom tex. ledarskap och personlig utveckling. Du kommer att coachas av en mentor på Axfood samt en konsultchef på Nexer Tech Talent. Efter framgångsrikt genomförande av talangprogrammet är intentionen att du erbjuds en anställning på Axfood.
   

  

**Vi söker dig som har:**
* Högskole-/universitetsexamen inom systemvetenskap, datateknik eller liknande område.
* Programmeringskunskaper i Python och SQL.
* Kunskaper inom AirFlow DBT och Terradata är meriterande.
* Förståelse för Business Intelligence/Analytics med datalakes, databaser etc.
* Intresse för molntjänster och serverless-arkitektur (AWS).
* Förmåga att arbeta med både frontend och backend.
* Goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska.


**Som person ser vi att du har följande egenskaper:**
* God teknisk förståelse.
* Självständig och ansvarstagande i ditt arbete, samtidigt som du är en lagspelare med god samarbetsförmåga.
* Nyfiken och driven i din egen kompetensutveckling.
* Öppen och lösningsfokuserad i ditt arbetssätt.
* Strukturerad och noggrann.


**Ansökan**
 Talangprogrammet startar i Augusti 2026 och riktar sig till dig som är nyexaminerad inom IT/tech. Vi arbetar löpande med rekryteringen, så skicka in din ansökan snarast möjligt. Om du har du frågor eller funderingar är du välkommen att kontakta Tech Recruiter Molly Rytter på molly.rytter@nexergroup.com
   

  

 Vi ser fram emot att höra från dig!
   

  

**Om Nexer Tech Talent**
 Nexer Tech Talent är en del av Nexer Group med väletablerade ramavtal, långsiktiga samarbete och några av Sveriges mest välkända och betydelsefulla kunder. Tillsammans är vi över 2500 medarbetare i 16 länder som i mer än 35 år hjälpt våra kunder ligga steget före både strategiskt, teknologiskt och kommunikativt. Läs mer om oss här.
   

  

**Om Axfood**
 Axfood grundades år 2000 och har visionen att vara ledande inom prisvärd, bra och hållbar mat. Koncernen består av en familj av varumärken och är unik i sitt slag på den svenska marknaden. I Axfood ingår butikskedjorna Willys, Hemköp, Tempo och Handlar’n. Grossistförsäljning sker genom Snabbgross, och Dagab ansvarar för koncernens produktutveckling, inköp och logistik. I Axfood ingår även Middagsfrid och Urban Deli samt delägda Apohem, Eurocash, City Gross och Mathem.
   

  

 Hos Axfood finns trygghet och stora utvecklingsmöjligheter. Medarbetarnas olika kunskaper, kompetenser och perspektiv skapar nya möjligheter och genererar bättre beslut. Genom att erbjuda en arbetsplats som speglar det samhälle vi lever i vill Axfood skapa ett öppet klimat där alla medarbetare har möjlighet att utvecklas. Axfood växer och utvecklas tillsammans vilket gör det möjligt att leda vägen och utmana en hel bransch. Välkommen till en familj av möjligheter! Läs mer om Axfood här.

title: Senior Software Engineer - Embedded Linux Platform
description:
10% of Europe's CO₂ emissions are caused by residential heating, and energy bills are out of control. Aira is here to change that.
   

  

 With our intelligent Aira Heat Pump at the heart, we're making the switch to clean energy-tech easier and more affordable. Helping Europe turn off gas and slashing energy costs one home at a time.
   

  

 Founded in 2022, and with 1000+ employees and counting, Aira is driving the clean energy revolution by providing homes with modern clean energy-tech solutions such as solar power, battery storage solutions and heat pumps. With a state-of-the-art production facility in Poland, an HQ in Stockholm, Sweden, and hubs across the UK, Germany, and Italy, Aira is well on track to install clean energy-tech into 5 million homes by 2034.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Linux Developer, you will be responsible for the operating system powering our IoT devices, ensuring optimal performance, security, and reliability. You will work with Linux BSP development and Yocto, building and maintaining the embedded platform as well as SDKs for application development.
   

  

 The role includes enabling secure connectivity across multiple wireless interfaces and supporting feature-rich, connected products. What sets this role apart is the opportunity to work with cutting edge technology in this field, with a direct impact on the performance, security, and reliability of our IoT devices.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
* Set up and configure the Yocto Linux-based operating system on our hardware devices.
* Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand software requirements and implement necessary changes.
* Create SDKs and software tools to facilitate application development on the operating system.
* Enhance and expand the operating system by adding new components and features.
* Conduct system revisions and optimizations to improve performance, security, production test flow, and user experience.
* Understand hardware schematics and identify requirements for driver development.
* Collaborate with hardware engineers to develop and adapt the operating system to the hardware.
* Continuously improve automated test coverage and devops system.


**What You Bring To The Team**
* Experience in Linux operating system development, Yocto, Shell Scripting and Python experience is preferred.
* Knowledge of Kernel development and experience with device tree.
* Ability to read hardware schematics and understand when driver development is necessary.
* Proficiency in C
* Solid understanding of software development principles, version control, and debugging techniques.


**Nice to have**
* U-Boot, Thread, Rust, AWS, Bluetooth Low Energy.


**What is in it for you?**
* Gain hands-on experience in a fast-growing scale-up, with a supportive and inclusive culture, and career advancement opportunities.
* Collaborate with diverse teams across international markets and launch products globally.
* Develop and grow personally and professionally in an entrepreneurial-minded environment.
* Thrive in a dynamic, solution-oriented culture, tackling challenges and driving meaningful solutions that make a positive impact on the world.
* Influence the company's success, with a visible impact on product development and market growth.


 At Aira, We’re not just champions of next-generation energy, we’re champions in Diversity and Inclusion too. We believe that a diverse workforce sparks innovation and creativity and enables us to better serve our customers and communities. We encourage individuals from all backgrounds, cultures, and perspectives to join our team. We will never accept any form of discrimination and believe that together, we can drive a new era for our people and planet.
   

  

**Ready to find true purpose in your work?**
 Send me your application today. Any questions? I’m here to help. You’ll find my contact details below.

title: Senior Embedded Software Engineer - Energy Storage Systems
description:
Lyten is leading an industrial revolution through Lyten 3D Graphene™, a breakthrough supermaterial unlocking a new generation of products — from lithium–sulfur batteries and energy storage systems to concrete admixtures, lightweight composites, and next-generation sensors that are revolutionizing industries. Together, these innovations are making a massive global improvement and driving real-world impact across energy, mobility, construction, and defense.
   

  

 At Lyten, we believe the most meaningful careers begin with purpose — and with people who want to make a difference. We’re not just developing advanced supermaterials — we’re about to change the world as we know it, reshaping how energy is stored, how products are built, and how progress is made.
   

  

 We’re entering an exciting growth phase, scaling production across the U.S. and Europe and expanding our team of engineers, scientists, and innovators.
   

  

 Apply now to join our team and be part of something bigger than yourself — where collaboration, creativity, and purpose come together to build the technologies that will define the next century.
   

  

 At Lyten, we’re building the next generation of energy storage technologies that will power a safer, more sustainable future. As a
 **Senior Embedded Software Engineer** 
 , you will play a critical role in designing and delivering the core firmware that runs on our Battery Management Systems (BMS) software that directly impacts safety, performance, and reliability at scale.
   

  

 The role is available in both Gdańsk and Stockholm, where you will work at the intersection of hardware, systems, and data, collaborating closely with hardware engineers, system architects, and data scientists to transform complex requirements into robust, safety-critical embedded software.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Own the architecture, design, implementation, and verification of core BMS firmware.
* Develop high-reliability embedded software in C, compliant with functional-safety standards.
* Design firmware modules and interfaces for cell monitoring, balancing, SOC/SOH estimation, and protection algorithms.
* Optimize CPU, memory, and power usage to meet real-time constraints.
* Debug complex hardware-software interactions across MCU peripherals (ADC, SPI, I²C, UART, Ethernet).
* Drive software quality through unit testing, automated regression, code reviews, and static analysis (MISRA-C).
* Produce clear technical documentation: design specs, interface definitions, and release notes.


**What You Bring**
* 8+ years of embedded software development experience, including 4+ years in battery-related systems.
* Deep understanding of microcontroller architectures and real-time embedded systems.
* Proven experience implementing battery-management algorithms (cell balancing, SOC/SOH, protection).
* Expert-level C programming skills (MISRA-C:2004/2012 compliance is a strong plus).
* Hands-on experience with Git, CI/CD pipelines, and static analysis tools.
* Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to debug complex, cross-disciplinary issues.
* A B.Sc. or M.Sc. in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related field.
* Fluent English, written and spoken.


**How You Succeed Here**
* You take full ownership — from concept through delivery — and care deeply about the impact of your work.
* You thrive in fast-paced, international environments and enjoy close collaboration across teams.
* You’re curious, pragmatic, and motivated by seeing your work used in real products by real customers.
* You value quality, flexibility, and continuous improvement, and you bring positive energy (and a sense of humor) to the team.


**Why Join Lyten**
* Be part of a company shaping the future of energy storage
* Play a pivotal role in expanding Lyten’s European presence


**Why Work at Lyten**
 At Lyten, you’ll be part of a team that’s redefining what’s possible in energy, materials, and manufacturing. We’re not just imagining the future — we’re building it today with breakthrough technologies that are changing how products are made and how industries innovate.
   

  

 Our people are the heart of that mission. From world-class scientists and engineers to creative problem-solvers in operations, manufacturing, and commercialization, every member of the Lyten team plays a role in turning bold ideas into real-world impact. We believe that the best work happens when you’re doing great things in the world — with people you like. Collaboration, curiosity, and a shared sense of purpose drive everything we do.
   

  

 What You’ll Find at Lyten
   

  

* A mission that matters: Contribute directly to solving complex challenges in energy, mobility, and materials innovation.
* Cutting-edge innovation: Work on technologies at the intersection of materials science, energy storage, and advanced manufacturing that strengthen energy security and local supply chains.
* Extraordinary people: Join a team of talented, friendly, and down-to-earth innovators who support, challenge, and inspire one another every day.
* Teamwork and culture: Experience a workplace built on trust, respect, and shared success — where collaboration fuels breakthroughs and everyone’s ideas are heard.
* Global impact: Help scale new materials and energy solutions that reinforce industrial resilience across the U.S. and Europe.
* Career growth: Be part of a fast-moving company entering a commercial growth phase, with opportunities to lead, learn, and make your mark.
* Purpose-driven values: Thrive in an environment that celebrates ingenuity, optimism, and meaningful progress — together.


 Lyten offers the opportunity to do the most important work of your career — helping build the technologies that will power the next century of innovation.
   

  

 Join us, and help transform industries, communities, and the planet with friends who share your drive to make a difference.

title: Engineering Manager, Observability Dept | Sweden | Remote
description:
Grafana Labs is a remote-first, open-source powerhouse. There are more than 20M users of Grafana, the open source visualization tool, around the globe, monitoring everything from beehives to climate change in the Alps. The instantly recognizable dashboards have been spotted everywhere from a NASA launch and Minecraft HQ to Wimbledon and the Tour de France. Grafana Labs also helps more than 3,000 companies -- including Bloomberg, JPMorgan Chase, and eBay -- manage their observability strategies with the Grafana LGTM Stack, which can be run fully managed with Grafana Cloud or self-managed with the Grafana Enterprise Stack, both featuring scalable metrics (Grafana Mimir), logs (Grafana Loki), and traces (Grafana Tempo).
   

  

 We’re scaling fast and staying true to what makes us different: an open-source legacy, a global collaborative culture, and a passion for meaningful work. Our team thrives in an innovation-driven environment where transparency, autonomy, and trust fuel everything we do.
   

  

 You may not meet every requirement, and that’s okay. If this role excites you, we’d love you to raise your hand for what could be a truly career-defining opportunity.
   

  

 This is a remote position. We are looking for candidates in the NASA (EST-only) or EMEA time zones.
   

  

**What is Grafana Cloud?**
 Grafana Cloud is our composable observability platform that integrates metrics, logs, traces, and profiles with Grafana. It allows our customers to leverage the best open source observability software – including Prometheus, Mimir, Loki, Tempo, and Pyroscope – without the overhead of installing, maintaining and scaling their own observability stack.
   

  

 The Observability department is focused on enabling developers to understand the health and performance of their applications and infrastructure in any environment by providing tools to instrument their code, ingest observability data into Grafana Cloud and visualize and explore it.
   

  

**What is Grafana Alloy?**
 Alloy is a distribution of the OpenTelemetry collector. Its purpose is to collect, process, and export telemetry signals at scale. Alloy combines the strength of leading ecosystems like OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Loki, Pyroscope, and more. Alloy is an open source project that is maintained on Github.
   

  

**What You'll Be Doing**
* Manage and develop the team of Alloy engineers, providing regular feedback and supporting each person’s growth through career conversations. This is a remote team, distributed between EMEA and NASA.
* Support the Alloy team on their path to closer collaboration with the OpenTelemetry collector community. Address roadblocks.
* Collaborate closely with go-to-market to ensure customers and prospects are successful with their Alloy journey.
* Work closely with product and engineering leadership to ensure Alloy fits seamlessly into the end-to-end user experience for Grafana cloud products.
* Provide guidance throughout the project lifecycle from early ideation through planning, execution and post‑launch guiding the team to deliver high‑quality results consistently.
* Foster a psychologically safe environment where engineers can learn, experiment and iterate quickly, encouraging innovation and a culture of continuous improvement.
* Work across team boundaries by contributing to projects outside your team’s scope and partnering with other functions to solve customer problems.


 As a company we are remote-first and global, we embrace people of different experiences and backgrounds to build diverse teams where every person brings a new perspective to the software.
   

  

 We invest heavily in developer productivity. You can use modern AI coding assistants as part of your daily workflow (your choice of tools, within security guidelines), backed by a company-funded usage budget so you can iterate quickly without unnecessary friction. We encourage pragmatic AI-assisted development: faster prototyping, test generation, refactors, documentation, and incident follow-ups—always paired with strong code review and quality standards. You’ll also have access to frontier models (e.g., GPT-Codex 5.4, Claude Opus 4.7, Gemini 3 Pro).
   

  

**What are we looking for in you?**
* You should have enough domain knowledge in observability and OpenTelemetry to manage a team that will collaborate closely with the OpenTelemetry collector community.
* You will be comfortable working with engineering teams who have a strong sense of autonomy in their decision-making, be it technical or product focused.
* While you’re great with people and adept at managing relationships, you still keep up-to-date with the latest technical trends and shifts in order to maintain and enhance your understanding of the challenges your teams face.
* Comfortable collaborating across time zones and cultures in a fully remote environment, using clear, empathetic communication to build trust, connection, and psychological safety.
* Demonstrated ability to manage complexity, align stakeholders, and elevate team performance to deliver meaningful, high-quality results.
* Deeply customer-focused, consistently grounding decisions and technical choices in user needs and real-world impact.
* Solid understanding of distributed systems principles, including scalability, fault tolerance, consistency, and observability.


 Comfort with AI-assisted tooling. We embrace AI and agentic development so we expect you to be curious and comfortable using AI-powered tools and ideally have practical experience folding them into a team’s workflow.
   

  

 In Sweden the Base compensation range for this role is SEK 878,578 - SEK 1,054,000 Actual compensation may vary based on level, experience, and skillset as assessed in the interview process. Benefits include equity, bonus (if applicable) and other benefits listed here.
   

  

* Compensation ranges are country specific. If you are applying for this role from a different location than listed above, your recruiter will discuss your specific market’s defined pay range & benefits at the beginning of the process.


**Why You’ll Thrive At Grafana Labs**
* 100% Remote, Global Culture - As a remote-only company, we bring together talent from around the world, united by a culture of collaboration and shared purpose.
* Scaling Organization – Tackle meaningful work in a high-growth, ever-evolving environment.
* Transparent Communication – Expect open decision-making and regular company-wide updates.
* Innovation-Driven – Autonomy and support to ship great work and try new things.
* Open Source Roots – Built on community-driven values that shape how we work.
* Empowered Teams – High trust, low ego culture that values outcomes over optics.
* Career Growth Pathways – Defined opportunities to grow and develop your career.
* Approachable Leadership – Transparent execs who are involved, visible, and human.
* Passionate People – Join a team of smart, supportive folks who care deeply about what they do.
* In-Person onboarding - We want you to thrive from day 1 with your fellow new ‘Grafanistas’ to learn all about what we do and how we do it.
* Balance is Key - We operate a global annual leave policy of 30 days per annum. 3 days of your annual leave entitlement are reserved for Grafana Shutdown Days to allow the team to really disconnect. \*We will comply with local legislation where applicable.


**Equal Opportunity Employer:** 
 We will recruit, train, compensate and promote regardless of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, disability, age, veteran status, and all the other fascinating characteristics that make us different and unique. We believe that equality and diversity builds a strong organization and we’re working hard to make sure that’s the foundation of our organization as we grow.
   

  

*Grafana Labs may utilize AI tools in its recruitment process to assist in matching information provided in CVs to job postings. The recruitment team will continue to review inbound CVs manually to identify alignment with current openings.*
*For information about how your personal data is used once you’ve applied to a job, check out our privacy policy.*

title: Senior Software Engineer (Golang)
description:
**WHO WE ARE** 





 At Trustly, we're building a smarter, faster, and more secure financial future by revolutionizing the world of payments. As a global leader in Open Banking Payments, we are establishing Pay by Bank as the new standard at checkout, providing unparalleled freedom, speed, and ease to millions of consumers and merchants worldwide.
 




  






 Our Ambition: To build the world’s most disruptive payment network and redefine what the payment experience should feel like.
 




  






 Trustly is a global team of innovators, collaborators, and doers.  If you are driven by a strong sense of purpose and thrive in a dynamic, entrepreneurial, and high-growth environment, join us and be part of a team that’s transforming the way the world pays.
 




  




 ABOUT THE TEAM
   


 At Trustly, we aren’t just processing payments; we are building the world’s most resilient financial network. The Transfer & Funds team is the engine room of this mission. We are the architects and guardians of Trustly’s financial integrity, responsible for the core systems that ensure instant, reliable settlements for millions of users worldwide.
 


  


  


 We are currently undertaking a massive evolution of our infrastructure: transitioning our core financial logic from a high-volume Postgres monolith into a clean, Go-based distributed architecture. We are looking for a Senior Engineer who thrives on technical complexity and wants to leave a legacy by building our next-generation transactional systems.
 


  

 Why Join Transfer & Funds?
   


 This is a "change the engines while the plane is flying" opportunity. You will work on the most critical systems at Trustly, solving the industry's hardest problems in transactional consistency and scale. If you enjoy deep technical puzzles and want to own the bedrock of a global fintech leader, we want to hear from you.
 


  


 Apply now and help us architect the future of global payments.
 


  






  




**WHAT YOU'LL DO** 
  

  
* **Modernize the Core:** 
 Play a lead role in moving mission-critical logic out of a centralized Postgres monolith and into a high-velocity, domain-driven environment
  
* **Build for 99.99%:** 
 Architect and maintain the systems responsible for our
 **Transfer System and Transactional Ledger** 
 , ensuring absolute data integrity and high availability
  
* **Solve Distributed Complexity:** 
 Design services that handle high-concurrency and global scale, ensuring that every transaction is atomic, consistent, and resilient to failure
  
* **Define Target Architecture:** 
 Contribute to the long-term technical vision of how Trustly handles money movement, balancing the need for rapid feature delivery with the rigor of financial accounting
  

  


  

  

**The Tech Stack** 
  

  
* **Primary:** 
 Golang, PostgreSQL.
  
* **Architecture:** 
 Distributed Microservices, Event-Driven Patterns.
  
* **Infrastructure:** 
 AWS, EKS, Terraform, Datadog.
  

  


  

  


  




**WHO YOU ARE** 
  

  
* **Strong Go Expertise:** 
 You have
 **5+ years** 
 of experience building robust, production-grade backend systems in
 **Golang** 
 . You write idiomatic code and have a deep understanding of Go’s concurrency model and performance characteristics.
  
* **System Design & Distributed Architecture:** 
 You understand the nuances of distributed systems. You know how to build application-level safety nets, handle partial failures, and ensure "exactly-once" processing across service boundaries.
  
* **Relational Power-User:** 
 You have experience in SQL and relational databases, like
 **PostgreSQL** 
 . You understand transaction isolation, locking strategies, and how to refactor schemas under heavy production load.
  
* **Migration Experience:** 
 You’ve successfully moved logic out of legacy environments into modern architectures. You know how to bridge the old and the new without disrupting live operations.
  
* **High-Availability Focus:** 
 You design for failure. You value observability and have experience running mission-critical services in
 **AWS/Kubernetes** 
 environments.
  

  


  





  




  


**Our Fantastic Benefits**

 (varies by location)
 




 🌴
 **20 to 30 days of holiday** 
 to support a healthy work-life balance
 



 🥳
 **Monthly team outing allowance** 
 to enjoy social events with your colleagues
 



 👶
 **Parental leave top-up** 
 additional support for new parents
 



 🥐
 **Daily breakfast and on-site perks** 
 to make your workday smoother
 



 💚
 **Well being support** 
 our health allowance covers gym memberships, massages, and much more to help you feel your best
 



**PLUS** 
 additional benefits designed to enhance your work-life experience!
 



  





**Shape Your Role and Make an Impact**




 Join a dynamic environment where you can take ownership, drive change, and continuously embrace new challenges. We’re looking for dedicated and highly motivated individuals who thrive in a fast-paced environment and enjoy collaborating across different areas of the organisation.
 



 If your skills and experience align with this role, we’d love to hear from you!
 **Apply now and submit your CV in English.** 



  


 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Frontend-utvecklare
description:
Är du en lösningsorienterad Frontend-utvecklare med öga för detaljer? Motiveras du av att bygga hållbara och tillgängliga webbplatser samt har ett intresse för den senaste tekniken? Vill du dessutom vara en del av ett bolag med en stark företagskultur där du får vara med och påverka, utveckla och bidra? Då kan rollen som Frontend-utvecklare hos oss på Svedea vara nästa utmaning för dig!
   

  

**Om tjänsten**
 Som Frontend-utvecklare blir du en del av ett engagerat team som arbetar agilt enligt Scrum. Du kommer att ha ett nära samarbete med backend-utvecklare och UX/UI-designers för att utveckla moderna och användarvänliga webblösningar som förenklar vardagen för våra kunder. Du är också en del av vårt webbskrå - en grupp webbspecialister som delar erfarenheter, utforskar nya tekniker och håller varandra uppdaterade på det senaste inom webbutveckling. Tillsammans tar ni idéer från tanke till verklighet, påverkar tekniska beslut och driver utvecklingen framåt.
   

  

 I rollen kommer du att:
   

  

* underhålla och vidareutveckla våra webbplatser med fokus på användarvänlighet och tillgänglighet
* arbeta med Blazor, SASS, Umbraco och Azure - DevOps
* bidra till ett levande designsystem tillsammans med våra UX/UI-designers
* säkerställa att våra tjänster är tillgängliga enligt WCAG 2.2 och EN 301 549


**Din bakgrund**
 På Svedea lägger vi stor vikt vid personlighet och potential. Vi söker dig som är en prestigelös lagspelare med god kommunikationsförmåga. Du är nyfiken, strukturerad och kreativ - du planerar ditt arbete väl, håller deadlines och slutför det du påbörjar. Det är naturligt för dig att se nyttan för både verksamheten och kunden i det du bygger.
   

  

 Andra krav för tjänsten är att du:
   

  

* har erfarenhet av C# och webbutveckling i frontend-miljö
* har kunskap om digital tillgänglighet och WCAG
* är van att arbeta tätt med UX/UI-designers
* är flytande i svenska och har mycket goda kunskaper i engelska, såväl tal som skrift


 Det är meriterande om du:
   

  

* har erfarenhet av Blazor och/eller Umbraco
* är van att utveckla och arbeta i designsystem
* känner dig trygg i agila arbetssätt


**Vi erbjuder dig**
