title: Senior Embedded Software Engineer
description:
Xylem is a Fortune 500 global water solutions company dedicated to advancing sustainable impact and empowering the people who make water work every day. As a leading water technology company with 23,000 employees operating in over 150 countries, Xylem is at the forefront of addressing the world's most critical water challenges. We invite passionate individuals to join our team, dedicated to exceeding customer expectations through innovative and sustainable solutions.
   

  

 As a Senior Embedded Software Engineer, you will contribute to the development of control systems used in pumping automation. These systems play an important role in ensuring reliable operation, high energy efficiency, and long-term value for our customers - from machine level to cloud connectivity.
   

  

 In this role, you will collaborate closely with product owners, system engineers, and test engineers, contributing across the full development lifecycle — from requirements and architecture to implementation, verification, and deployment. The role also includes hands-on work in our lab, as well as occasional visits to customer sites to better understand real-world applications.
   

  

 While this is not a managerial role, you will have the opportunity to act as a technical mentor, contribute to best practices, and participate in technical discussions that help shape the direction of the team.
   

  

 You will be part of a collaborative team where knowledge sharing and continuous learning are valued.
   

  

**Key Responsibilties**
* Design, develop and maintain embedded software for control systems in our products
* Contribute to software architecture, technical decisions and long‑term maintainability
* Develop robust and testable solutions using modern embedded software practices
* Collaborate cross‑functionally with product owners, test engineers and other R&D teams
* Drive continuous improvements in ways of working, tools and code quality


**Qualifications**
* Experience in embedded software development using C / C++
* Experience working with real-time operating systems (e.g., Zephyr or similar)
* Understanding of software architecture and design for embedded systems
* Experience with unit testing, static code analysis, and quality-focused development practices
* Experience with version control systems such as Git and collaborative development workflows
* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience
* Good communication skills in English
* Knowledge of Swedish is considered a plus


**Personal Profile**
 We believe you bring a curious mindset and enjoy working with complex technical challenges. You are comfortable taking initiative in your work while also valuing collaboration and shared problem-solving.
   

  

 You communicate clearly and enjoy engaging in technical discussions with others. You appreciate working in a team environment where different perspectives and approaches are welcomed, and where continuous learning and improvement are part of everyday work.
   

  

**Why Work at Xylem!**
 We offer you a creative and international environment where you get the opportunity to focus on your personal and professional development together with committed and helpful colleagues. Engage as a volunteer through Xylem´s business program for community initiatives Xylem Watermark: www.xylemwatermark.com.
   

  

**Everyone's equal value**
 We want people, regardless of skin color, age, disability, sexual orientation, and gender identity, to feel at home, not just at Xylem, but everywhere in the world. For us, diversity, equality, and inclusion mean more than just a policy or a set of methods. Diversity, equality, and inclusion are fundamental parts of our corporate culture and a key to long-term growth.
   

  

 Feel free to visit us at https://www.xylem.com/sv-se/
   

  

**Application**
 If you are interested in this position and would like to know more, please contact Leila Sayyad, Corporate Recruiter,
 **Leila.sayyad@xylem.com** 
 .
   

  

 Welcome with your application!
   

  

 Join the global Xylem team to be a part of innovative technology solutions transforming water usage, conservation, and re-use. Our products impact public utilities, industrial sectors, residential areas, and commercial buildings, with a commitment to providing smart metering, network technologies, and advanced analytics for water, electric, and gas utilities. Partner with us in creating a world where water challenges are met with ingenuity and dedication; where we recognize the power of inclusion and belonging in driving innovation and allowing us to compete more effectively around the world.

title: Agentic AI Solution Specialist (Cloud & Custom Applications)
description:
Att välja Capgemini innebär att välja en arbetsplats där du ges möjlighet att forma din karriär på det sätt du själv önskar, där du ingår i en stödjande och inspirerande gemenskap med engagerade kollegor världen över, och där du får chansen att omdefiniera vad som är möjligt. Våra Cloud & Custom applications-team fokuserar på att utveckla skräddarsydda cloudapplikationer, integrera IT-system och driva affärstransformation, med leverans av affärskritiska cloudlösningar i hög kvalitet och hastighet, möjliggjort av Generative AI. Bli en del av oss och hjälp världsledande organisationer att frigöra teknikens värde och bygga en mer hållbar och inkluderande framtid!
 



  





**Din roll** 




  





 Som Agentic AI Solution Specialist blir du en drivande kraft i att forma framtidens intelligenta automation. Du kommer att arbeta nära våra kunder för att identifiera affärsmöjligheter och omsätta dem till robusta, agentbaserade AI-lösningar. Rollen är konsultativ och kombinerar tekniskt djup med affärsmässighet – du är både rådgivare och inspiratör. Utöver arbetet med kunder blir också en viktig del av vårt interna kompetensnätverk genom kunskapsdelning, pre-sales och strategiska initiativ. Eftersom rollen är ny får du stor frihet att påverka dess utveckling och forma vägarna framåt för att skapa verkligt värde för Capgemini och våra kunder.
 



  





*I den här rollen kommer du att ha en nyckelroll i:* 




  




* Driva kundprojekt inom Agentic AI och intelligent automation från idé till leverans.
* Agera senior advisor i strategiska initiativ och projekt som rör Agentic AI, och bidra med expertstöd i komplexa frågor.
* Utveckla och presentera go-to-market-förslag samt delta i pre-salesaktiviteter.
* Bygga och dela kunskap internt genom Capgeminis Guilds och Centers of Excellences, samt kombinera teknisk expertis med affärsdrivande och kommunikativa insatser för att inspirera och sälja in lösningar.



  





**Din profil** 




  




* Dokumenterad kompetens inom Agentic AI och erfarenhet av att omsätta komplex teknik till affärsvärde.
* Djup teknisk expertis kombinerad med affärsmässighet och en konsultativ, kommunikativ och pedagogisk approach – du kan förklara teknik på ett sätt som skapar förståelse och engagemang.
* Förståelse för att förändringsinitiativ handlar om människor lika mycket som teknik – du skapar engagemang och säkerställer adoption av nya lösningar.
* Flytande kunskaper i engelska och svenska i tal och skrift



  





**Vad vi tror kommer få dig att trivas** 




  




* Möjligheten att forma en pionjärroll med stor frihet och påverkan på dess utformning – du blir en nyckelperson i att driva intelligent automation och Agentic AI hos våra kunder och internt.
* Tillgång till den senaste tekniken och plattformar som Microsoft Copilot, Azure AI foundry, UiPath Agentic och Google Cloud, samt ett globalt nätverk av experter.
* En kultur som uppmuntrar innovation, kompetensutveckling och kunskapsdelning, där du får utrymme att leda förändring och skapa verklig impact.



  





**Ansökan** 




  





*Efter att du skickat in din ansökan gör vi en första urvalsbedömning. Därefter följer en telefonintervju med ansvarig Talent Acquisition Partner, en intervju med rekryterande chef på kontoret och en teknisk intervju där vi går djupare in på din kompetens. Som avslutning genomför vi relevanta tester och referenstagning innan vi går vidare till ett eventuellt erbjudande.* 




  





*Vi värdesätter din tid och ser till att du får tydlig kommunikation och snabb återkoppling genom hela processen. Du kommer löpande kunna ställa frågor och få uppdateringar från ansvarig Talent Acquisition Partner – vårt mål är att skapa en positiv upplevelse där du får en rättvis bild av både rollen och oss som arbetsgivare.* 




  





*Som en del av vår rekryteringsprocess kommer identitetskontroll att genomföras genom granskning av ditt ID-kort, och bakgrundskontroller kan utföras vid behov.* 




  





**Om Capgemini** 




  





 Capgemini är en AI-driven global partner för affärs- och tekniktransformation som levererar konkret verksamhetsnytta. Vi formar framtiden för organisationer och förverkligar den med hjälp av AI, teknik och människor. Med vår starka historia på nästan 60 år är vi en ansvarsfull och mångsidig koncern med över 420 000 medarbetare i över 50 länder. Genom vår expertis inom strategi, teknik, design, ingenjörstjänster och affärsverksamhet levererar vi heltäckande tjänster och lösningar, baserade på vår djupa branschkunskap och vårt omfattande partnernätverk. Koncernen rapporterade globala intäkter på 22,5 miljarder euro för 2025.
 



  





**Make it real** 
 | www.capgemini.com

title: Agentic AI & Automation Engineer to Handelsbanken
description:
* Application Deadline: May 11, 2026
* Stockholm
* On-site
**Job Description**
-------------------

Join Handelsbanken’s AI & Automation department and help shape how we apply Agentic, Generative and Predictive AI at scale across the bank.  

  



**Your team**  

The Agentic AI & Automation Team is part of Handelsbanken’s AI & Automation department, which leads the bank’s AI transformation. Our team is focused on developing next-generation solutions that combine AI agents, automation and advanced analytics to transform how the bank operates and serves customers.


We work across the organization to develop high-impact solutions in areas such as customer experience, operations and decision-making. You will be joining a team with strong momentum, high ambitions and the opportunity to shape a new capability area from the ground up.


**Your role**  

As an Agentic AI & Automation Engineer, you will be at the forefront of combining automation and AI to build intelligent, autonomous solutions that solve real business problems. You will work end-to-end — from understanding business needs to designing, building, deploying and improving solutions in production.


Your work will include:


* Building AI and automation solutions with focus on Agentic AI and Generative AI
* Leveraging both UiPath capabilities and the bank’s AI platform
* Driving end-to-end solution development together with a Product Manager:
	+ Understanding business problems and requirements
	+ Designing and developing solutions
	+ Testing and validating outcomes
	+ Deploying and productionizing solutions
	+ Monitoring, maintaining and continuously improving solutions
* Collaborating closely with business stakeholders, engineers and other AI specialists

This is a highly hands-on, impact-driven role where you will take ownership and help define how Agentic AI and automation are applied across the bank.


**Great Place to Work**
-----------------------

* Tjänstepension
* Resultatandelssystem
* Gruppförsäkring
* Friskvårdsbidrag
* Bidrag vid föräldraledighet
* Banktjänster
* Banking services
* Benefit in connection with parental leave
* Health promotion subsidy
* Group insurance
* Occupational pension
* Profit-sharing scheme
**Skills & Requirements**
-------------------------

**Your profile**  

We are looking for someone early in their career with strong technical fundamentals, curiosity and a drive to learn by building real solutions. You are a fast learner with a natural curiosity and a strong interest in exploring new technologies such as Generative AI and automation. You thrive in a supportive, fast-moving environment where you can develop your skills while contributing to real projects. You take ownership of your tasks, actively seek feedback, and enjoy collaborating with both technical and business teams. Since we collaborate across the organisation, strong communication skills, in Swedish, are important.


Above all, you are motivated by learning and contributing to solutions that create real, measurable impact over time.


**Requirements:**

* Master’s degree or equivalent in a technical or quantitative field (e.g. Engineering, Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics)
* Strong problem-solving skills and interest in building solutions with code
* Advanced knowledge of at least one programming language for data science (preferably Python)
* Basic understanding of applied statistics and/or mathematics
* Ability to explain technical concepts in a clear and structured way
* Interest in understanding business processes and how technology can support them
* A proactive mindset and strong willingness to learn

Experience in one or more of the following is highly valued:


* Generative AI, Predictive AI and/or Agentic AI solutions
* Automation development (e.g. UiPath or similar tools)
* Building production-ready AI or software solutions
* Working across the full lifecycle from idea to production

**Welcome with your application**

*As part of Handelsbanken’s recruitment process, candidates who progress to the next stage will be asked to complete a logical reasoning and personality test. The final candidate is also required to undergo and successfully pass a background check conducted by an external provider before employment can be offered.*

**Contact details**

**Recruiting manager:** Jovan Zamac, jovan.zamac@handelsbanken.se
  

**Talent Acquisition Partner:** Alva Jonerot, alva.jonerot@handelsbanken.se
  

**Union representative Finansförbundet:** Per Almshult, per.almshult@handelsbanken.se
  

**Union representative Akademikerföreningen:** Bjarne Nilsson, bjarne.nilsson@handelsbanken.se


  
**About the company**
---------------------

At Handelsbanken, it’s meetings between people that are the foundation of everything we do. We believe in long-lasting relationships with both customers and employees, and always put what’s best in the long term ahead of any short-term interests.


With confidence in our employees and respect for their individual capacities, we delegate responsibilities as far out into the organisation as possible. Whatever role you have, you are a part of the decision-making process. With us, you will grow with responsibility. There are many career paths, but you make your own journey. We value attention to detail, understanding, initiative and development. Each employee’s experiences are unique and contribute to the big picture. Together, we are Handelsbanken.


Mer om Handelsbanken Mer om Handelsbanken

title: Junior Systemverifierare
description:
*Saab is a place where teamwork makes things fly. Literally. Join us and experience our supportive and friendly workplace.*


Your role


We are seeking a Junior System Verification Engineer to join our growing team. In this role, you will play a critical part in ensuring the quality and functionality of our systems, with a primary focus on system and Graphical User Interface (GUI) testing.


We are looking for a candidate who can design, develop, and execute comprehensive test plans and test cases. The role involves utilizing various testing methodologies, including black box, white box, and exploratory testing, to identify and report defects. The candidate will collaborate closely with development teams to understand software requirements and translate them into testable scenarios. After testing, results will be analyzed and clear test reports compiled. The person will also contribute to the continuous improvement of testing processes and methodologies. Experience in writing system requirements and breaking them down into detailed test cases is a plus.


Your profile


The ideal candidate holds a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field, and has 1-3 years of proven experience in system testing. A solid understanding of system engineering is required, along with excellent analytical and problem-solving skills. Strong communication and interpersonal abilities are essential, as well as the capability to work both independently and collaboratively within a team.


For this position you need to have excellent communication skills in both Swedish and English.


Preferred Qualifications:  

Experience in writing system requirements and breaking them down into detailed test cases is highly valued. Familiarity with test management tools, such as Jira, is also preferred.


This position requires that you pass a security vetting based on the current regulations around/of security protection. For positions requiring security clearance additional obligations on citizenship may apply.


What you will be a part of


Explore a wealth of possibilities. Take on challenges, create smart inventions, and grow beyond. This is a place for curious minds, brave pioneers, and everyone in between. Together, we achieve the extraordinary, each bringing our unique perspectives. Your part matters.


Saab is a leading defense and security company with an enduring purpose, to help nations keep their people and society safe. Empowered by its 28,000 talented people, Saab constantly pushes the boundaries of technology to create a safer and more sustainable world.


Saab designs, manufactures and maintains advanced systems in aeronautics, weapons, command and control, sensors and underwater systems. Saab is headquartered in Sweden. It has major operations all over the world and is part of the domestic defense capability of several nations. Read more about us here
company_description: Saab serves the global market with world-leading products, services and solutions from military defense to civil security.

title: System Design Engineer – Vehicle Connectivity - F/M/X
description:
Job description
  
We are looking for a skilled System Design Engineer with a strong background in Electrical & Electronics engineering to join a team working on Vehicle Connectivity systems. You will play a key role in supporting system development, improving connectivity functions, and ensuring high-quality requirement management within a complex automotive environment.
  
  

**Location:** Gothenburg, Sweden
  
**Experience Level:** 4–6 years
  
**Language:** English (Fluent)
  
  

Your profile
  
Experience in system design within automotive or connectivity domains (4–6 years)
  
Knowledge of vehicle connectivity systems and automotive architecture
  
Knowledge of CAN signaling and basic diagnostics
  
Experience in requirements management tools (e.g., SystemWeaver)
  
**Academic background:** BSc or MSc in Electrical Engineering, Software Engineering, or a related field
  
English fluent. Swedish is a plus.
  
You demonstrate strong stakeholder management and communication skills
  
You have a T-shaped mindset with willingness to learn and expand across domains
  
  

Your missions
  
Support the Product Owner and System Owner within the Fleet Management Systems (FMS) area
  
Collaborate with Function Owners to report maturity, progress, and status of End User Functions (EUF)
  
Drive continuous improvement initiatives within the connectivity domain
  
Provide technical support and take ownership of assigned connectivity areas
  
Manage and develop system requirements in line with established processes
  
Ensure effective stakeholder communication and alignment
  
Contribute to system design, development, and verification activities
  
  

What we offer
  
An international community, bringing together 110+ different nationalities
  
**An environment where trust has a central place:** 70% of our key leaders started their careers at the first level of responsibilities
  
A robust training system with our internal Academy and 250+ available modules
  
A vibrant workplace that frequently gathers for internal events (afterworks, team buildings, etc.)
  
At Mantu, sustainability is part of everything we do. You’ll have the opportunity to turn your ideas into action and make a tangible impact. Every day, our teams bring our ESG commitments to life, from reducing our footprint to driving positive change within our communities. Through our WeCare Together program, you’ll be empowered to design and lead projects that create real social or environmental impact, with the company’s full support.
  
  

**Salary range:** From 46 667 SEK to 56 667 SEK (gross, per month)
  
  

The stated salary falls within the established salary range for the position and is determined based on experience, skills, and level of responsibility, in accordance with our policy of fair and non-discriminatory compensation.
  
  

Amaris Consulting is proud to be an equal-opportunity workplace. We are committed to promoting diversity within the workforce and creating an inclusive working environment. For this purpose, we welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, beliefs, age, marital status, disability, or other characteristics.
  
Who are we?
  
Amaris Consulting is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. With more than 1000 clients across the globe, we have been rolling out solutions in major projects for over a decade – this is made possible by an international team of 7,600 people spread across 5 continents and more than 60 countries. Our solutions focus on four different Business Lines: Information System & Digital, Telecom, Life Sciences and Engineering. We’re focused on building and nurturing a top talent community where all our team members can achieve their full potential. Amaris is your steppingstone to cross rivers of change, meet challenges and achieve all your projects with success.
  
  

At Amaris, we strive to provide our candidates with the best possible recruitment experience. We like to get to know our candidates, challenge them, and be able to give them proper feedback as quickly as possible. Here's what our recruitment process looks like:
  
  

**Brief Call:** Our process typically begins with a brief virtual/phone conversation to get to know you! The objective? Learn about you, understand your motivations, and make sure we have the right job for you!
  
  

Interviews (the average number of interviews is 3 - the number may vary depending on the level of seniority required for the position). During the interviews, you will meet people from our team: your line manager of course, but also other people related to your future role. We will talk in depth about you, your experience, and skills, but also about the position and what will be expected of you. Of course, you will also get to know Amaris: our culture, our roots, our teams, and your career opportunities!
  
  

**Case study:** Depending on the position, we may ask you to take a test. This could be a role play, a technical assessment, a problem-solving scenario, etc.
  
  

As you know, every person is different and so is every role in a company. That is why we have to adapt accordingly, and the process may differ slightly at times. However, please know that we always put ourselves in the candidate's shoes to ensure they have the best possible experience.
  
We look forward to meeting you!
company_description: Amaris Consulting is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. It gathers more than 7,500 people across 5 continents and more than 60 countries.

title: System Verification Engineer (Expert) - F/M/X
description:
Job description
  
We are looking for a System Verification Engineer to join an in-house software development team within the Electrical & Software Architecture domain.
  
This role is ideal for someone passionate about testing, verification, fault tracing, and software quality, with a hands-on approach and strong problem-solving mindset.
  
  

**Location:** Gothenburg, Sweden
  
**Language:** English (Fluent)
  
**Experience:** 4–6 years
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
Perform V3 testing in HIL rigs and BoxBus environments
  
Conduct fault tracing and root cause analysis
  
Design, develop, execute, and report test cases based on system requirements
  
Support bug fixing and validation activities
  
Ensure software quality and release readiness
  
Collaborate with cross-functional teams and contribute to continuous improvement
  
  

Required Qualifications
  
BSc or MSc in Electrical Engineering, Software Engineering, or equivalent
  
4–6 years of experience in system testing and verification
  
  

**Strong experience with:**  

Python and/or CAPL programming
  
CANoe / CANalyzer tools
  
HIL (Hardware-in-the-Loop) testing environments
  
Experience in fault tracing and debugging
  
Familiarity with CI/CD environments and AI-driven workflows
  
Ability to work independently with a proactive mindset
  
  

Preferred Qualifications
  
Experience in the automotive industry
  
Exposure to bodybuilder interface systems (BBM, BIM, BIO)
  
Previous experience with CAN-based communication systems
  
Background in similar large-scale engineering environments
  
  

Soft Skills
  
Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills
  
Self-driven with ability to take initiative
  
Collaborative mindset with strong communication skills
  
T-shaped profile (broad knowledge with deep expertise in key areas)
  
  

What we offer
  
An international community, bringing together 110+ different nationalities
  
**An environment where trust has a central place:** 70% of our key leaders started their careers at the first level of responsibilities
  
A robust training system with our internal Academy and 250+ available modules
  
A vibrant workplace that frequently gathers for internal events (afterworks, team buildings, etc.)
  
At Mantu, sustainability is part of everything we do. You’ll have the opportunity to turn your ideas into action and make a tangible impact. Every day, our teams bring our ESG commitments to life, from reducing our footprint to driving positive change within our communities. Through our WeCare Together program, you’ll be empowered to design and lead projects that create real social or environmental impact, with the company’s full support.
  
  

**Salary range:** From 46,667 SEK to 56,667 SEK
  
  

The stated salary falls within the established salary range for the position and is determined based on experience, skills, and level of responsibility, in accordance with our policy of fair and non-discriminatory compensation.
  
  

Amaris Consulting is proud to be an equal-opportunity workplace. We are committed to promoting diversity within the workforce and creating an inclusive working environment. For this purpose, we welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, beliefs, age, marital status, disability, or other characteristics.
  
Who are we?
  
Amaris Consulting is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. With more than 1000 clients across the globe, we have been rolling out solutions in major projects for over a decade – this is made possible by an international team of 7,600 people spread across 5 continents and more than 60 countries. Our solutions focus on four different Business Lines: Information System & Digital, Telecom, Life Sciences and Engineering. We’re focused on building and nurturing a top talent community where all our team members can achieve their full potential. Amaris is your steppingstone to cross rivers of change, meet challenges and achieve all your projects with success.
  
  

At Amaris, we strive to provide our candidates with the best possible recruitment experience. We like to get to know our candidates, challenge them, and be able to give them proper feedback as quickly as possible. Here's what our recruitment process looks like:
  
  

**Brief Call:** Our process typically begins with a brief virtual/phone conversation to get to know you! The objective? Learn about you, understand your motivations, and make sure we have the right job for you!
  
  

Interviews (the average number of interviews is 3 - the number may vary depending on the level of seniority required for the position). During the interviews, you will meet people from our team: your line manager of course, but also other people related to your future role. We will talk in depth about you, your experience, and skills, but also about the position and what will be expected of you. Of course, you will also get to know Amaris: our culture, our roots, our teams, and your career opportunities!
  
  

**Case study:** Depending on the position, we may ask you to take a test. This could be a role play, a technical assessment, a problem-solving scenario, etc.
  
  

As you know, every person is different and so is every role in a company. That is why we have to adapt accordingly, and the process may differ slightly at times. However, please know that we always put ourselves in the candidate's shoes to ensure they have the best possible experience.
  
We look forward to meeting you!
company_description: Amaris Consulting is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. It gathers more than 7,500 people across 5 continents and more than 60 countries.

title: Embedded engineer in Sweden
description:
Are you interested in working in a high-tech environment at multiple OEMs, while still maintaining a good work/life-balance? Keep on reading!
  
Welcome to Europe’s most thriving automotive high-tech cluster
  
In Gothenburg there are not less than 4 automotive OEMs, and together with the tier one suppliers we are positioned in the center of Europe’s most thriving automotive high-tech cluster. MCA have projects with 20+ different companies which create a vast number of possibilities for you as an MCA consultant.
  
This is an on-site position, but don’t worry, Sweden is a great place to live! We are consistently ranked among the top 10 countries in the World Happiness Report, with a high level of social security and big focus on work/life-balance. On top of this, the tech scene in in Sweden are extremely hot, with everything from the high-tech automotive OEMs to a thriving start-up culture, so you will have all the chances to have a stimulating career development.
  
So, if you want a thrilling career in the tech-dense city of Gothenburg surrounded by beautiful nature, great work culture and still with time over for your private projects and hobbies keep on reading!
  
The job
  
You will work as a consultant for one of our many exciting clients. You will be involved in the development of new, state-of-the-art automotive software platforms that will enable groundbreaking progress in the fields of autonomous drive, connected vehicles and electromobility to mention a few.
  
Most of our clients work in agile cross functional teams. The team has full ownership of its features. This means that you will be involved in all stages of development, from design, implementation, and testing.
  
We offer you the chance to work with cutting-edge technology, with the support of our dedicated consultant managers who will help you take your career to new heights!
  
The ideal profile
  
We want you to be curious and engaged, ready to take on new challenges and contribute to the development at our clients. You have great skills in communication and a flexible mindset. You need an engineering master and relevant working experience, preferably within embedded software.
  
  

**Qualifications:**  

Master’s degree in electrical engineering, computer science, mechatronics or similar
  
5+ years of experience from embedded software development
  
Fluent in English
  
EU citizen and EU drivers license
  
  

If you find this opportunity interesting, please apply as soon as possible!

title: EDS system engineer
description:
**Professional Galaxy** is an IT and technology consulting company that provides highly specialized expertise within IT, software development, SAP, purchasing, electronics and mechanical design. We collaborate with experienced senior experts and deliver strategic value-creating expertise to some of Sweden's most complex and analytically demanding projects. Our focus is always on high quality, professionalism and clear, measurable results.


We are now seeking a **EDS system engineer** for one of our clients.

**About the assignment:**


We are looking for a motivated and curious engineer who wants to develop within Electrical


Distribution Systems (EDS) in the automotive industry.


This is a great opportunity for someone with a technical foundation who wants to learn more about


wiring harnesses, fuse strategies, grounding concepts and complete vehicle electrical architecture


while working in an exciting vehicle development environment.


In this role you will:

* Support development of the vehicle electrical distribution system
* Work with wiring harnesses, distribution boxes, wire sizing and fuse concepts
* Assist in system design based on vehicle loads and functional requirements
* Collaborate with function, system and component owners
* Participate in supplier communication and technical follow-up
* Participate in the onsite plant build support across Europe and/or China
* Support analysis of changes and impacts when new functions are introduced
* Support analysis of quality matters reported by customers
* Report progress and status within the project team.


Qualifications and skills required for the role

* Master degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering or similar
* Basic understanding of electrical systems or automotive technology
* Understanding of EU customer expectations or market needs
* Strong willingness to learn and grow within EDS
* Structured way of working and ability to take ownership
* Good communication skills
* Fluent English, spoken and written


Preferred qualifications:

* Interest in automotive electrical architecture
* Experience from automotive industry or supplier environment
* Knowledge in schematics or electrical calculations.

**Personal attributes**


We believe you are:

* Curious and eager to learn
* Driven and proactive
* Responsible and structured
* Positive team player
* Interested in solving technical challenges.

**Uppdragsinformation**  

Uppdragslängd: 2026-08-01 to 2027-04-30

Svar önskas snarast, dock senast 2026-05-03


Are you the right person for the assignment, or do you want to recommend a strong candidate? Do not hesitate to contact us.


Please apply directly through our system with:

* Your updated CV in english
* Availability to start the assignment


In the motivation, describe why you are suitable for this assignment - refer to previous consulting assignments, employmxent, education and personal qualities.

**Please note:** We do not accept any applications through mail. All applications have to be sent through the portal to be valid.

**Offer continuously:** Please note that for this role we offer continuously. That means that we sometimes remove the assignments before the deadline. If you are interested, we recommend that you apply immediately.

title: Junior QA Engineer
description:
### **About FirstVet**


FirstVet is the first truly digital veterinary clinic. We let pet owners meet with licensed veterinarians via their mobile or computer — and we don't stop there. We've expanded to include an e-commerce platform, offering a comprehensive solution for pet owners across 7 markets in Europe and North America. Our mission is simple: to make quality veterinary care accessible to every pet and pet owner, wherever they are. We're a tech-driven company at heart, and the engineering team plays a central role in how we deliver on that mission. If you're passionate about building reliable, high-quality software and want to do it in a place where your work genuinely matters, we'd love to hear from you.

### **The role**


This is a role designed to grow with you. You'll start by supporting our product teams with hands-on QA work and grow into driving how we approach quality more broadly as you build experience.


You'll be joining a small, mixed team focused on enabling the product teams — which means real ownership and broad exposure across everything we do. While the team is small, you'll work closely with experienced engineers across our four product teams, so you'll have plenty of people to learn from and collaborate with as you take on bigger problems. We're in an exciting phase where the business continues to grow and scale internationally, while at the same time we're focused on maturing and hardening the technological foundation we've built. Our current focus is on operational excellence: strengthening our quality practices and ensuring our services are as robust and reliable as they can be. Alongside the day-to-day, we'd love for you to bring your own perspective — identifying gaps and proactively driving improvements.

### **Your responsibilities**

* Help maintain and improve the testing tooling, frameworks, and quality practices that allow our product teams to develop and ship their services with confidence
* Contribute to our ongoing quality and reliability work, helping teams catch issues earlier and ship with confidence
* Develop and improve our end-to-end test suites, as well as other quality-related automation tasks
* Maintain and evolve our shared testing component library, internal QA tooling and test data infrastructure
* Drive initiatives to improve our test coverage and quality foundation, enabling teams to better understand the health of their services
* Proactively identify areas for improvement and take ownership of driving them forward
* Take on hands-on QA work on behalf of the product teams, helping them put QA processes and frameworks in place

### **What success looks like**


In your first months, you'll get hands-on with testing work across our product teams — both automated and manual. As you build more experience we'd expect you to start driving your own initiatives to evolve and improve our testing processes and frameworks.

### **The person we are looking for**


We are looking for a junior QA Engineer who is curious, collaborative, and genuinely excited about taking ownership. You don't need to have all the answers on day one — what matters more is that you're motivated to learn, you spot things that could be better, and you do something about them. You're comfortable supporting engineers across different teams and enjoy being the person others can lean on for quality-related work. When you encounter a complex problem, you dig in, take ownership, and see it through to a solution.

### **Desired qualifications**

* Some hands-on experience with software testing, whether manual, automated, or both
* Familiarity with test automation frameworks such as Playwright, Cypress, Selenium or similar
* Experience with or exposure to CI/CD pipelines and an understanding of how testing fits into them
* Programming or scripting skills in a language such as TypeScript, Python, PHP or similar
* Experience with or a keen interest in different types of testing, such as API testing, end-to-end testing or similar
* A collaborative and communicative approach to working with engineers across teams
* Fluency in English, written and spoken

### **What we offer**


At FirstVet, you'll be joining a team that genuinely cares about what it's building and how it builds it. We value collaboration, trust, and giving people the space to do their best work. This role comes with real ownership from day one and plenty of room to grow. We offer a highly flexible, hybrid remote work environment — work from our Stockholm office or remotely, it's up to you. And of course, it goes without saying that an interest in animals is highly desirable. We're a pet-oriented organisation and it's not unusual to run into our office dogs or for a cat to join our Hangout calls!

title: System Verification Engineer
description:
Build the Future of Technology with **Professional Galaxy AB**  

Professional Galaxy AB is an IT and technology consulting company providing highly specialized expertise in IT, software development, SAP, procurement, electronics, and mechanical design. We collaborate with experienced senior experts and deliver strategic, value-driven competence in complex and analytically demanding projects. Our focus is always on high quality, professionalism, and clear, measurable results.


We are now looking for a **System Verification Engineer**

**Beskrivning**  

Are you a passionate and skilled verification engineer who wants to work with the new technology impacting the future of our group? We offer you the opportunity to join us in developing the very best energy storage solutions on the market.


Who are we?  

We develop world-class electromobility solutions for our products. You will not only get to work with cool technical challenges – but also be part of an optimistic atmosphere where joy, knowledge-sharing, and openness are key characteristics. In our team you will be a part of work with energy storage system testing for our various battery products.


What you will do  

In this role you will be in the heart of battery technology, working with verification of batteries ensuring fulfillment of safety, performance, and functional requirements. Testing will be done to understand the characteristics and functionalities of complete battery packs configurations.


The work is done in close collaboration with development teams, suppliers, and other functions. You therefore need to be skilled in working in a cross-functional and international environment.


We are seeking experienced verification engineers who will have the following responsibilities:  

* Verify battery packs and their functionality and performance
* Prepare rig environment for testing
* Increase automation of testing procedures
* Develop test methods and test requirements
* Develop ways of working within ESS verification area
* Plan, perform, report, and follow up on verification deliverables

Requirements:  

* B.Sc. or M.Sc. in Power Electronics, Electrical and Electronics or similar relevant education
* At least 10 years of system testing experience and at least 3 years of experience in battery development and/or testing of battery packs or modules
* Good communication and co-operation skills are essential in this role
* Experience with Python, Matlab and Vector Tools

Meriting:  

* Experience from commercial vehicle or automotive product development
* Experience in testing environments for battery packs
* Experience in battery testing in vehicle environment
* Truck driving license

What we can offer  

We are offering you the opportunity to be a part of one of the biggest technology shifts in the transport industry. Electromobility is changing the way we travel, commute and transport goods. In our different teams of committed and diverse engineers we are fighting the environmental challenges of the future. By joining us, you will be able to contribute with your skills and knowledge alongside be an active participant in developing test methods for our future battery platforms.

**Uppdragsinformation**  

Uppdragslängd: 2026-05-01 to 2026-11-30  

Placeringsort: Göteborg

**How to Apply**  

Are you the right person for this assignment, or do you want to recommend a strong candidate?  

Please apply via the Professional Galaxy AB process with:  

* Your updated CV
* Your availability to start
* A short motivation describing your suitability, including relevant experience and background

title: Engineering Manager – Monetisation
description:
**YOUR MISSION AT VOI**  

As an Engineering Manager at Voi, you will lead and grow a cross-functional product team while staying close to the code. This role is ideal for a strong technical lead ready to take a first step into people management – someone who wants to shape team culture, support engineers’ growth, and drive delivery without leaving their technical roots behind.


The Monetisation team owns Voi’s core payment flows, subscriptions, upsell and in-app advertising. The technical scope spans core backend systems, payment integrations, native mobile client views and more. This is an excellent opportunity to grow into leadership while having a tangible impact on the micromobility industry.

* Be a present, hands-on leader for a team of 4–5 engineers: run 1:1s, support growth and create a safe, collaborative environment where people do their best work.
* Partner closely with the Product Manager and Designer to align on priorities, shape the roadmap and drive high-quality delivery.
* Lead by doing: write code, review PRs and engage deeply in technical decisions across backend, web and mobile.
* Champion engineering excellence by guiding architectural decisions, fostering good development practices and promoting continuous improvement – enabling sustainably rapid delivery in a complex micromobility business.
* Coach and develop engineers through feedback, mentorship and clear expectations – with support from us if this is your first formal people management role.
* Lead the team in adopting agentic engineering practices: actively use and encourage AI-assisted development and help engineers build the habits and judgement to get the most out of these tools.
* Contribute to a collaborative engineering culture aligned with Voi’s values, and be a driver for inclusion and psychological safety within your team.

**WHAT YOU NEED TO EMBARK**  

You are a hands-on, collaborative engineer with a passion for both technology and people. You enjoy working across domains, take ownership of delivery and are curious about how AI can elevate the way teams build software.

* 4+ years of hands-on experience as an engineer in modern tech stacks, building reliable software in a fast-paced environment.
* A generalist mindset with experience in backend, web and/or mobile development.
* 2+ years of experience in a tech lead, senior or informal leadership capacity – you have guided others technically, even if you have not held a management title.
* No prior people management experience required – this is a growth area we will support you in.
* Practical experience with AI-assisted development tools (for example modern code assistants) and a genuine interest in agentic engineering workflows. You lean into these tools and help your team build the skillset to use them effectively.
* Strong communication skills and the ability to navigate both technical and cross-functional conversations with ease.
* Fluency in English, written and spoken.

*Don’t meet every requirement? If you are excited about this role and our mission, and have relevant, transferable skills, we encourage you to apply – even if you don’t check every box.*

**WHY VOI?**  

Working at Voi is more than just a job; our People Promise includes a personal voyage where you will grow as a professional and be a part of a team and culture that builds something meaningful for society. In addition to this you’ll have the opportunity to:

* Join Europe’s #1 micromobility operator and one of the fastest growing scale-ups.
* Get “skin in the game” through our employee options programme.
* Enjoy unlimited free Voi rides and a dog-friendly office.
* Work together with inspiring, motivated and fun colleagues towards a common goal.
* Join the micromobility revolution and be a part of creating sustainable cities made for living, free from noise and pollution.

title: Staff Engineer | Group Operations
description:
**Every parcel we deliver starts with an engineering decision**


At Instabee, we don't just move parcels — we solve some of the most complex physical-world problems in European logistics, at scale. From the algorithms that optimize a driver's route to the systems that predict terminal throughput hours in advance, our engineering decisions have a direct impact on millions of deliveries every day.


We're now looking for a Staff Engineer to join our technical leadership group — someone who doesn't just write great code, but shapes how entire teams think, build, and deliver.

### **About the Role**


This isn't a traditional engineering role. As a Staff Engineer in Group Operations, you sit at the intersection of technical strategy and real-world logistics. You work alongside Product and Engineering Management to ensure multiple teams are building the right things, in the right way, at the right scale.


Think of yourself as the technical glue — the person who bridges business ambition and engineering execution, who spots the hard problems before they become expensive ones, and who raises the bar for the entire engineering organization around you.

### **What You'll Do**

* **Drive Technical Strategy & Architecture** Set the technical direction across multiple teams in Operations. You'll guide engineers toward right-sized solutions — balancing high-scale stability with the speed needed to move the needle on operational KPIs.
* **Tackle Genuinely Hard Problems** Forecasting incoming parcel volumes, optimizing terminal throughput, routing thousands of drivers in real time — these are the kinds of challenges that define our business and your days.
* **Champion Developer** Experience Identify and eliminate technical friction. You'll lead initiatives to improve testing environments and deployment speed, keeping our engineering organization sharp and efficient.
* **Bridge Business & Tech** Translate complex business requirements into evolvable technical designs. You'll help teams understand how their code directly impacts the P&L of our logistics network — and why that matters.
* **Mentor & Build Culture** Act as a role model for the engineering community. You'll mentor senior engineers, promote a culture of learning from failure, and foster cross-team collaboration to ensure we don't reinvent the wheel.
* **Enable Data-Driven Decisions** Work closely with Data and Product teams to ensure our systems produce high-quality insights that power company-wide decisions.

### **What We're Looking For**


You don't need to be an expert in everything your teams do — but you should care deeply about helping them succeed. We think you'll thrive in this role if you are:

* **A Technical Leader** — You have broad experience building distributed systems and can navigate deep technical dives and high-level architectural trade-offs with equal confidence.
* **Strategic & Pragmatic** — You know that "perfect is the enemy of good." You favor an iterative mindset, delivering value early and often rather than waiting for the ideal solution.
* **Operationally Curious** — You're fascinated by the intersection of software and physical operations. Experience in logistics, routing, or hardware integration is a significant plus.
* **A Communicator & Collaborator** — You can translate complex technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders and thrive in a cross-functional environment where engineering, product, and business work closely together toward shared goals.
* **A Quality Advocate** — You hold a high bar for stability and security — but you know how to achieve it without killing the pace of innovation.

### **Why join Instabee?**


You won’t just be another employee here; you’ll be a builder. We offer the chance to sharpen your skills alongside some of the brightest minds in the industry. If you’re someone who sees an inefficient process and thinks, "I can make that better," you’re going to love it here.

### **The Perks:**


The Best View in Town Our Stockholm HQ offers a stunning 360-degree panorama.


Dog-Friendly Culture No need to leave your best friend at home; our furry friends have their own floor!


Unwind & Recharge Whether it's a competitive game of Foosball or a Karaoke solo, we take our fun seriously.


Family First We support our new parents with six months of parental pay.


Birthday Gift Enjoy a fully paid day off to celebrate another trip around the sun.


Work-Life Harmony Hybrid working and flexible hours come as standard.

### **The Journey So Far**


Instabee was born when two pioneers, Budbee and Instabox, joined forces. Today, we are on a mission to become the leading European e-commerce enabler. We are a high-speed business, expanding across borders and constantly evolving. As our journey grows in scale and complexity, we need people like you to help us navigate the road ahead.

*This recruitment process consists of background checks via Verifiera as a review of previous public criminal records. The result is public information that is handled confidentially. This is mandatory for all recruitment processes at Instabee in Sweden.*

title: Powertrain Engineer
description:
**Want to shape the future of waterborne transport and join a world-class team?**


Candela is the **global leader in electric hydrofoil vessels**. Since 2014, we’ve gone from building our first premium electric speedboat in a small Stockholm workshop to delivering game-changing vessels around the globe. **The Candela P-12** is our latest innovation - the world’s first electric hydrofoil ferry is not just operating in Stockholm, but now attracting orders from around the world.  

Our boats “fly” thanks to **computer-guided** underwater wings under the hull made out of **lightweight carbon fiber.** The wings lift the vessel above the water, which results in cutting energy use by up to 80%, while offering zero emissions and a far better passenger experience than traditional diesel vessels.  

  

And that’s exactly why we do this. Our mission is clear: to **speed up the transition to fossil fuel–free lakes and oceans**.


We are now looking for a **Powertrain Engineer** to support Candela’s growth ambitions. In this role you will be driving the development for one or more components and functionalities in the Powertrain system throughout the complete lifecycle. We take responsibility and drives the development from concept through industrialization and all the way to our demanding customers.  

  

This is a team, that plays an instrumental role in the development of our vessels and consists of skilled engineers and experts with extended knowledge and experience of all critical high voltage components that is needed to make the boats fly. When joining Candela and the Powertrain team you will be part of a team that is in the forefront in the world of electrification.

**Your core responsibilities will include:**

* Own the Propulsion system, from charging inlet to electric machines, as well as cabin HVAC and low-voltage actuators.
* Define and set requirements for system, system behavior and components.
* Secure that the system can be certified according to applicable standards.
* Evaluate and test performance, functionality and quality of the system.
* Interact with and support the software team to help achieve ultimate functionality and performance of the control systems.
* Support after market and service to ensure the best possible customer experience.
* Support production and procurement to secure products with competitive quality and cost.

 **What are we looking for?**


We’re looking for a future team member with experience and motivation to work with electrical components, prior experience of high voltage batteries, fast charging high voltage applications and powertrain system. The new team member shouldn't be just technically skilled, but also a creative mind to push ideas further.

**Requirements:**

* Master’s degree in engineering or similar
* You have prior experience, education or strong interest in high voltage powertrains and an engineering degree in a relevant field.
* You have fundamental knowledge of electrical rotational machines, motor controller topologies or batteries.
* You have system experience and a good understanding of how to integrate components into a system realizing complex functionality.
* Self propelled with a strong drive and feel confident to work in a dynamic environment with high pace, shifting focus and strong market presence.
* Excellent communication skills in English and team-oriented attitude

**Form of employment**: Full-time with initial 6 month probation period  

**Start date:** ASAP  

**Location/base**: Frihamnen,
  

**Team**: Powertrain Engineering  

**Reporting to**: Director Software & Powertrain Engineering

**Why people love working at Candela?**  

At Candela, we’re not just building electric vessels - we’re creating a whole new category of clean, efficient waterborne transport. You get the chance to work with **cutting-edge technology**, solving complex challenges, and seeing the results in action - sometimes literally, when we organize test rides for our newcomers! We’re a diverse and ambitious team of over 50 nationalities, united by a **revolutionary spirit and kindness**. That means we work hard, but always with humility and while having fun - whether it’s solving a manufacturing challenge, improving processes, or cheering each other on during **Candela Months**, our year-round calendar of bonding activities and events.  

  

We’re **office-first but not office-only**. We believe collaboration is most powerful when we build together, in person - but we also trust our team to work in ways that fit their life. At Candela, you’ll find a place where bold ideas are welcomed, great people are celebrated, and the ride is just as important as the destination.  

  

**Recruitment process:**  

We aim to keep our recruitment process as straightforward and efficient as possible. As an international company with English as our corporate language, the entire process is conducted in English. Therefore, please submit your application in our corporate language as well. Kindly note that we cannot process applications sent via email due to GDPR so please use our career website for you application.

 **We welcome the opportunity to meet you!**

title: AI Software Engineer
description:
*Drivs du av att se dina idéer bli verklighet? Välkommen till vår spännande och teknikintensiva värld.*
**Din roll**
 Vi står inför ett teknikskifte där AI får en allt större betydelse i hur framtidens försvarssystem utvecklas. Därför bygger vi nu upp en verksamhet där AI är en naturlig del av hur vi designar, utvecklar, testar och tar ansvar för mjukvara över hela livscykeln. Du blir en del av uppbyggnaden av vår förmåga inom AI stödd mjukvaruutveckling på DBS med nära koppling till systemansvar produktkvalitet och säkerhet.
   

  

 I rollen arbetar du brett inom mjukvaruutveckling från design och implementation till test och verifiering med AI som ett aktivt stöd i arbetet. Du använder exempelvis AI baserade verktyg för att skriva analysera och förbättra kod och för att stödja beslutsfattande kring kvalitet robusthet och underhållbarhet.
   

  

 Du har ett tydligt helhetsansvar för den mjukvara du arbetar med hur den är uppbyggd hur den beter sig i drift och vilka konsekvenser designval får för säkerhet kvalitet och deployment. Även om plattformsteam ansvarar för underliggande infrastruktur behöver du ha tillräcklig förståelse för plattform och deployment för att kunna göra trygga tekniska val och samarbeta effektivt.
   

  

 En viktig del av rollen är att bidra till att utveckla arbetssätt och tekniska riktlinjer för hur AI kan användas på ett ansvarsfullt och säkert sätt i säkerhetskritisk mjukvara. Du är med tidigt vågar tänka nytt och bidrar aktivt till att forma hur vi arbetar både tekniskt och metodmässigt.
   

  

**Din profil**
 Du är nyfiken analytisk och trivs med att utforska nya lösningar. Du tar ansvar för mer än bara din egen kod och tycker om att resonera kring arkitektur kvalitet och långsiktighet. Att dela kunskap och utvecklas tillsammans med andra ser du som en självklar del av jobbet.
   

  

**Vi ser att du behöver ha:**
* Intresse för och kunskap om AI exempelvis LLMer agentbaserad AI eller autonoma verktyg
* Programmeringsvana i exempelvis Python Java eller C++
* Erfarenhet av mjukvaruutveckling professionellt eller genom egna projekt
* Förmåga att ta ansvar för kvalitet struktur och tekniska helheter
* Grundläggande förståelse för plattform deployment och driftsättning samt hur dessa påverkar mjukvarans beteende och säkerhet
* Vilja att samarbeta och bygga kunskap tillsammans med andra


**Meriterande:**
* Intresse eller erfarenhet av säkerhetskritiska system
* Förståelse för hur robusta och tillförlitliga system designas
* Erfarenhet av arkitekturarbete test CI CD eller samarbete med plattformsteam
* Egna experiment prototyper eller open source bidrag kopplade till AI


*Befattningen kräver att du genomgår och godkänns enligt vid var tid gällande bestämmelser för säkerhetsskydd. För befattningar där Saab har krav på säkerhetsklassinplacering kan, i förekommande fall, medföra krav på visst medborgarskap.*
**Vad blir du en del av**
 Här har du chansen att påverka och bidra på ditt unika sätt. Allt från att utveckla kod och bygga imponerande försvars- och säkerhetslösningar till att ta en kaffe med en kollega. Varje bidrag räknas. Vi ger dig möjlighet att anta utmaningar och skapa smarta innovationer. I vår vänliga och tekniksmarta värld finns plats för dig att växa. Vi finns här för att tillsammans skapa trygghet för människor och samhällen.
   

  

 Saab är ett ledande försvars- och säkerhetsföretag med uppdrag att hjälpa nationer att skydda sin befolkning och bidrar till trygghet för människor och samhällen. Med 27 000 talangfulla medarbetare utvecklar Saab teknik och lösningar för en säkrare och mer hållbar värld.
   

  

 Saab utvecklar, tillverkar och underhåller avancerade system inom flygteknik, vapen, ledningssystem, sensorer och undervattenssystem. Saab har sitt huvudkontor i Sverige men en global verksamhet där Saab är en del av många nationers försvarsförmåga. Läs mer om oss här.

title: Software Engineer – Embedded / Backend (ROS2) (Stockholm) - (all genders)
description:
**About Us:** 




 STARK is a new kind of defence technology company revolutionizing the way autonomous systems are deployed across multiple domains. We design, develop and manufacture high-performance unmanned systems that are software-defined, mass-scalable, and cost-effective. This provides our operators with a decisive edge in highly contested environments.
 



 We’re focused on delivering deployable, high-performance systems - not future promises. In a time of rising threats, STARK is bolstering the technological edge of NATO Allies and their Partners to deter aggression and defend Europe - today.
 



  





**Your mission:** 




  





 You will develop and integrate embedded and backend software that runs on our unmanned systems. The work ranges from implementing ROS2 components to supporting hardware bring-up and testing.
 



  





**Responsibilities:** 




  




* Develop embedded/back-end software modules for UAV systems
* Implement ROS2 nodes, interfaces, and integration logic
* Work on embedded Linux platforms and support hardware bring-up
* Debug issues across software, firmware, and hardware boundaries
* Support system testing and validation with hardware-in-the-loop in real life
* Collaborate with Autonomy, Flight Controls, and Hardware teams
* Contribute to clean, reliable code and clear documentation



  





  





**Qualifications:** 




  




* Experience in
 **C++** 
 development for embedded or robotics systems
* Solid background in
 **ROS2** 
 (nodes, topics, services, DDS)
* Familiarity with Linux-based development
* Understanding of sensors, communication protocols, and integration challenges
* Experience with robotics, UAVs, or other mission-critical systems is an advantage
* Hands-on mindset; comfortable testing on actual hardware
* Nice to have experience of Computer Vision, Sensor Processing and NVIDIA Jetson
* Excellent communication and stakeholder management experience
* Native/ Full professional proficiency Swedish or Norwegian language skills are a must have
* You must be eligible for Swedish security clearance (
 *Säkerhetsprövning* 
 ) and be willing to travel to Germany and other European locations.



  





  





**About us:** 




 We are an equal-opportunity employer committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, or any other characteristic protected by law. Due to the nature of our work in the defense sector, candidates must be eligible to obtain and maintain the appropriate security clearance required for the position.

title: Senior Software Engineer
description:
**Craft:**
**Job Description:**
 Want to create the world’s best mobile games?
   

  

 We’re looking for a Senior Software Engineer to join our Kingdom. Our games are adored by hundreds of millions of people, and you’ll be helping build them, as well as contributing to the engine, systems and platforms that make them play brilliantly on any platform.
   

  

 Your role within the Kingdom
   

  

 If you want to create amazing user experiences and entertainment for millions of players on the go, this is the right job for you. As part of the Central Technology team in Candy Crush Soda Saga, you will both design Soda’s long‑term architecture and bring it to life by implementing end‑to‑end systems directly in our client codebase. This role bridges big‑picture system design with hands‑on development, ensuring robust, maintainable, and high‑performance solutions. You’ll also be the one to deliver reference implementations that teams can immediately adopt across the codebase.
   

  

 Working closely with the tech leadership team, you will shape Soda’s technical strategy and ensure our architecture continues to evolve to support future success. Along the way, you’ll enjoy a creative, collaborative environment where your ideas are valued as much as your technical expertise.
   

  

 Your responsibilities will include:
   

  

* Auditing Soda’s architecture, designing new systems, and improving existing ones
* Taking full ownership of new systems from concept through production‑ready code, delivering examples for adoption across the client codebase
* Collaborating with the rest of the Technical Leadership in Soda to align on strategy
* Contributing high‑quality, well‑structured code to the client codebase
* Coaching colleagues and serving as a role model for technical excellence in areas like code quality, design, and collaboration


 Skills to create thrills
   

  

 We equally value a passion for making great games, extensive hands‑on coding experience, strength in software architecture, and a knack for sharing great ideas. You are:
   

  

* Highly proficient in system design, with proven experience architecting large and complex systems
* Very strong in C++, with experience working in large codebases
* Skilled at balancing architectural vision with pragmatic delivery, ensuring designs translate into production‑ready solutions
* Passionate about games and would love to help create the next big thing
* Driven by quality, polish, and pride in the solutions you deliver
* Proactive in taking technical ownership from design to implementation


**Bonus Points (nice To Have, Not Required)**
* Experience in the gaming industry
* Experience with Android or iOS development
* Experience working with memory‑ or performance‑critical applications
* Experience developing tools with scripting languages


**About King**
 With a mission of
 *Making the World Playful* 
 , King is a leading interactive entertainment company with more than 20 years of history of delivering some of the world’s most iconic games in the mobile gaming industry, including the world-famous Candy Crush franchise, as well as other mobile game hits such as
 *Farm Heroes Sag* 
 a. King games are played by more than 200 million monthly active users. King, part of Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT), has Kingsters in Stockholm, Malmö, London, Barcelona, Berlin, Dublin, San Francisco, New York, Los Angeles and Malta. More information can be found at King.com or by following us on LinkedIn, @lifeatking on Instagram, or @king\_games on X.

title: Senior Software Engineer
description:
**Join our highly skilled team of developers - breaking new ground and shaping the future of retail.** 




  





**We dare to dream big, we have the courage to keep raising the bar, and we’re committed to being a force for good in retail - helping both established and fast-growing brands meet the ever-changing expectations of their customers.** 




  





**Sitoo is a fast-growing technology company exploring how far we can take innovation to deliver the world’s best Unified Commerce Platform and Point of Sale. Our technology is used in more than 20 countries - a number that keeps growing thanks to our incredible team of engineers. And we have no intention of slowing down. Are you the Senior Software Engineer we’re looking for?** 




  





**Your mission at Sitoo** 




  





 As a Senior Software Engineer at Sitoo, you’ll be part of a small, autonomous team responsible for backend services that power our platform. This is not a role where you “hand over” code - you design, build, deploy, operate, and improve your services end-to-end.
 



  





 A big part of your work will be making thoughtful technical decisions: choosing the right patterns, improving system architecture, and ensuring that what we build is reliable, scalable, and maintainable over time. You’ll work primarily in AWS, using modern practices such as infrastructure as code, automated testing, and CI/CD to deliver quickly without compromising quality.
 



  





 You’ll also play an important role in how we evolve as an engineering organization - contributing to best practices, raising the bar for reliability and observability, and helping the team make sound trade-offs.
 



  





**What you’ll work with** 




  





 In this role, you’ll design and operate low-latency backend services, working across both synchronous APIs and asynchronous, event-driven systems. You’ll integrate with internal and third-party systems, and ensure that services behave well under real-world load.
 



  





 You’ll continuously improve how systems perform in production - reducing latency, increasing resilience, and strengthening observability through logging, metrics, and alerting. When incidents happen, you take part in understanding root causes and driving long-term fixes, not just quick patches.
 



  





 Security and efficiency are also part of the job: building systems with least-privilege access, secure defaults, and a conscious approach to AWS cost and performance.
 



  





**What you bring to the table** 




  





 You are an experienced engineer who takes ownership beyond just writing code. You care about how systems behave in production and continuously look for ways to improve them. Therefore, we believe that you have:
 



  




* Strong backend development experience with Node.js or similar
* Proven experience building scalable and reliable cloud services
* Solid understanding of distributed systems and asynchronous architectures
* Experience with infrastructure as code and modern CI/CD practices
* Experience designing and integrating APIs
* A product-oriented mindset with a focus on real user value



  





 Just as importantly, you bring a strong sense of ownership. You approach problems in a structured and pragmatic way, communicate clearly, and collaborate well with others. You care about building systems that not only work today, but continue to work tomorrow.
 



  





**Bonus points** 




  





 Experience with AWS services such as SQS, SNS, DynamoDB, Lambda, or Aurora will help you get up to speed quickly, as will familiarity with Docker or Kubernetes in production environments. If you’ve worked with high-traffic or low-latency systems, or have experience with identity, access management, or security in CI/CD pipelines, that’s a plus.
 



  





**Why Sitoo** 




  





 We are forward thinkers. By turning curiosity into knowledge - and knowledge into continuous improvement - we break new ground and shape the future of retail.
 



  





 We believe in an inclusive culture where everyone feels like they belong. We support each other, win together, celebrate success together, and overcome challenges together.
 



  





 Today, we are close to 115 dedicated experts with a positive and solution-oriented mindset. Most importantly, we take care of each other, and we never forget to have fun.
